quote: Growing up in my home town and knowing my father built a lot of my house, I know how the cost of housing has skyrocketed... The housing in my town is too high in price for someone who is retired or living on a fixed income... More affordable housing options would give them a sense of belonging in the community. It would make them want to be part of community activities and community groups.

The present cost of housing is extremely high. We need to be able for young newly married people to buy a small home instead of living with their parents. They can't even rent an apt for what they make working at fast food places... I now pay more for my apt than my son pays for his home... Seniors are priced out of their apts when they live a long time... Contractors need to not make so many neighborhoods with expensive homes. We could use some smaller houses.

I'm in a rent to own and the owners of the house won't fix anything... it's becoming a concern for my health. Damages can cause sickness, and mold to grow... I have Lupus and day by day things can be a struggle... I wish that there's programs to help renters and people that rent to own fix their houses so... their health and mental well being increase and in the long run save the state money on health care for low income and disabled families.

I would love to see minimum wage be increased so that a single person can afford a one bedroom apartment and all associated bills with only one job... I have been trying to report my work for health code violations and my landlord for tenet tenant right violations but they the path to do so is long and murky... We shouldn't have to suffer in silence just because we are an underserved community.

We need more jobs to be brought into western Oklahoma, to help raise our economy... It would raise us out of the poverty level in Oklahoma, which in return would bring a higher education ranking... and bring back rural towns... I'm from a small rural town... and I have watched my school lose its students and state funding and end up closing.

I hope that my daughter and I are more comfortable financially... to allow the occasional luxury such as a concert or other show, a vacation out of state, or the purchase of a luxury item without anxiety over cost. ... I feel that overall wages are extremely depressed in Oklahoma... Even a salary like mine that is more than double the minimum wage isn't always enough to cover the basics. ... Sadly, if people have to take on second and sometimes third jobs, they will be unable to engage with their neighbors, pursue their outside interests or even be there to engage with spouses and children.

I hope for job security and a decent wage to actually live in a home comfortablely... The rising cost of living obviously makes it harder... I'd like to see more small opportunities that could in turn help the environment and community... It could build relationships and provide a sense of achievement and a belonging to the community rather than just a person receiving help. People need a sense of belonging for there to be an active change.

I hope to have enough money to sustain me and my family that allows me to maximize my time with my family... I've built a career and family that I love and cherish... I've run into lots of negative external circumstances but working hard and believing things can change is a testament to this... Prioritize people and not profit... and attract high quality educators to the state or keep them here with competitive salaries.

These changes would make a real difference because they would give people a chance to get back on their feet instead of just surviving day to day. My personal experience was I've been homeless before... I was in the rain and snow sleeping in a tent without a blanket. My parents and my brother are currently homeless... and don't deserve to be laying in the middle of winter... in a tent like a dog not knowing when they will be able to shower or even eat.

Right now the main concern that I hope to be resolved is income. Currently just barely getting by with the job market as it is... I went to school here in Oklahoma... Despite our general education being lowly rated, at Votech they bragged that Oklahoma is one of the best states for votech and it showed. There's no reason why public schools can't be that way as well if they're funded and treated right.

The minimum wage in our state is extremely low compared to the cost of living... I would like to see an increase in wages to help more families out of poverty... There would be less crime and less people on snap and government assistance if they were able to afford the cost of living.

We all could use a better way to make money. I rely on internet connection for my very small amount of income, but even at a reduced price, it's difficult to...make each month's payment... It would free up that $25. to be used for something else, such as new boots, or school supplies for the boy.

Being able to pay bills on time is hard, but due to my health I can not work much... Where I live we need more businesses that will provide jobs. They also need to raise the minimum wage so people can actually survive; the cost of food is hurting a lot of people.

Don't let out of state companies buy properties to sub rent. All communities that charge rent should be owned by someone in the state of Oklahoma... As for reporting problems, I'm truly afraid I'll lose my home over it because the people involved were hired by the company who owns this place out of Michigan.

I would like to see the cost of living to decrease in the state. Things have gotten out of hand with prices... A year ago I thought a 1.89 Dr Pepper was expensive, now they're nearly 3.00. At this rate no one will be able to live. Plus, small owner businesses should be helped.

Being able to work more in my current field would help so I would have a better income... as a disabled vet, it is harder to do more of the manual labor jobs I'd like to do because of the pain they cause... I started some college but I didn't like the classes I took. I also live out where there isn't a lot of options or at least I don't feel like there are.

My boy... doesn't buy as many 'extras' as he used to... I would only hope that they could be less stressed with their daily lives... We need an improved minimum wage to actual cost of living ratio and housing and food security for families with children.

Making a better income at my current job would be a great way to set me up for future success. At my current workplace, they only give one yearly raise that is not enough to cover my expenses. I could certainly leave and find a better paying job; however, the positive environment at my workplace makes it difficult to want to leave... My goal for the future is to either start my own company or work in healthcare- two options that both are able to balance bringing in enough money and also provide a positive work experience for me.

The cost of living has moved faster than my income... There's just not much left after bills to buy groceries some months... I want to take care of myself. I do not want the government to take care of me... I do pick up food from the monthly food bank. Maybe, our people in government need to see what's going on at home.

A more stable economy and better wages for Oklahoma all-around. Our economy continues to decline because we are not a desirable state... I would like to see increased efforts or campaigns on the state level to bring in new revenue and attract new businesses.

I can't control the rent prices and I can't control the food prices. I just have to... learn to eat cheaper and have a garden... Knowing you have the extra food is a comfort and also you can help someone else if they need food or are hungry.

The job market is awful, and finding a well paying job is a nightmare. We are scraping to get by on basically pennies a day... I would like to see the state forbid and investigate workplaces for unlawfully black listing former employees to prevent them from getting jobs. The hospital in Altus, OK, is the worst for this.

So far my Bachelor's degree and nail technician school and license hasn't been able to provide a living wage.... Most of us are living just one $500 bill away from disaster. Yet, all I do is work... and no matter how much I work and try to save, I can never seem to get my head above water. I guess if there were some sort of support for people that were diagnosed as adults with autism... I really could have used that to help me find a viable career when I was young.

Our wants are small. Enough food, a roof, indoor plumbling. Really not a big 'ask'. However, this is still a function of money and as such is out of our control... The tariffs and immigration crack down have really hurt food production... I want to see local and state leaders listen to the constituents (for a change) and do what WE need, not what they need to get re-elected.

I hope that our financial situation would be more substantial... I would like to see more... available resources - especially to those who live outside our urban city limits... when I lived in a non urban area... it wasn't available.

I want a job that cares about their employees and notices their employees accomplishments and provides raises more often when the employees do what they are supposed to do, never late, does their job right, helps train other people when needed without being asked, never calls out of work, goes above and beyond what's expected.

I would like to be living in a home that belongs to me and not a landlord... Rent has increased to a point to where some people working can't afford to put a roof over their head. This is a real problem in small and large towns.

I personally retired 3 years ago and couldn't feed myself, so I went to work in a restaurant so I could eat there... Minimum wage needs to be higher. Let Oklahoma compete for the workforce instead of workers going out of state.

I could open up my own car shop... It's scary out there financially. Jobs pay super low and everything costs so much. This can create chaos in the mind leading to frustration that leads to giving up... I would reunite with my son. He lives in Texas... I think if I had more money, I could go to Texas and make him love me back.

If you are always working just to keep your lights on and food on your table, there isn't anymore money left over to save for a house or a car... We deserve clean and safe housing just like everyone else... but I'm currently on housing and every place available is always dirty, full of bugs, dilapidated, or in a very unsafe neighborhood.

I hope that jobs and income will be easier to come by and working 3+ jobs for one person shouldn't need to exist anymore... It's been difficult to get a job with new AI integration of everything, which makes it harder to earn income... Earning more income could help me eventually buy a house in the future.

I hope one day that me and my family can finally be homeowners... but it's unbearable; everything is expensive from rent to groceries... I would like to see more food pantries and more funding to different organizations that can help people in need with rent, utility and whatever other resources people need to survive.

I hope that me and my kids all own our own houses and are financially stable... What I can do to make this happen would be working a little harder, maybe a second job... I would like to see more... stores to shop at and food places to eat at with your family on the northeast side of town. I believe it would help the community a lot.

Our teachers are some of the worst paid in the country... I would also like to see the minimum wage raised to at least a livable amount for a small family... Year after year I have seen... teachers leaving for other states nearby, just to make a living wage. Being the worst state for teachers to work in is costing us and our children, and that cannot be made up later. We have the money; it’s just not being spent wisely.

I'd like to see the state lookout for poor people and fight against inflation and fight for poor people being dragged across the system... It's hard not getting hired... and not having enough jobs... in this bad economy.

I'd have to get 6 more jobs and 15 more side jobs just to get groceries... We need more job opportunities within communities with above average income... to grow a strong and thriving environment where everyone can flourish.

It's hard worrying about how I'm gonna feed all these people with only one pound of meat that costs me 7 dollars... If we had more reliable access to healthy staples, we would be more relaxed to spend money in the community to help out thriving businesses in our small towns.

I'm watching my brother and his wife struggling to support their kids. My sister-in-law is a teacher, and my brother is a school counselor with a masters degree, and he's having to work at Reasors part time so they can scrape by. I'm struggling to keep up myself as a teacher living on my own. It shouldn't be this difficult. How is life supposed to be worth living when all we ever do is struggle? ... This is not the life I was promised as a kid. I'm exhausted.

I hope that I am more financially stable... I would like to see an increase in the pay on minimum wage. Also the town of Arkoma needs a lot of work ... make more businesses... we need a grocery store.

We need more help for elderly and disabled to keep from being kicked out of their homes because of delinquent property tax (like me getting ready to lose my home). ... Make monthly property tax payments possible for those of us on fixed income; it's hard to try to save $2,000 to send when monthly online payments would help most of us

Making more money than I do now would create a better tomorrow. It's out of my control because my place of business gives a yearly raise that equals to about three percent... I used to door dash as a side hustle, so I will need to pick that back up and do that in my spare time.

We have families where both parents are working and they still struggle to get bills paid or put food on the table... I honestly think our state wouldn't rank where we do with mental health issues and depression if families weren't stressed out about finances.

It's beginning to be hard to afford basic every day items... I would be able to focus on training and learning a new skill... We need higher paying jobs so we don't have to struggle living pay check to pay check... and programs to learn about AI.

The thing that could help me get a better tomorrow is more pay... Higher income means that I would have less cut off notices... I would like to see everyone get the same opportunity to become better rather than just the same group... I live in south OKC, and we donâ€™t have as much opportunity... as someone who lives in Nichols Hills.

I hope to be paying off all credit cards, so we can be more financially independent and be able to purchase a new final home... I would like to see the state help by lowering sales taxes to avoid burden on the less likely to afford this, giving them more money to provide for themselves and their family.

I would hope that myself and my children will be living in a state that rewards those in the helping professions... I have worked in Oklahoma in education and for DHS in Child Welfare. Our state does not provide enough resources for those who are teaching and protecting our children.

The high prices on groceries and property taxes and high insurance rates on auto and house are out of my control... Seniors could stay in there home and not be worried how to afford groceries, medications and upkeep on there home.

Currently we struggle a lot and I think it would be great to no longer have to worry... I would like to see lower gas prices. The price really takes a toll when you travel a lot. If we could lower groceries as well, it would be great. We could travel to see family way more often... or have others over for dinner far more often.

We want to increase our income... but the cost of living needs to come down. From food cost, gas prices and taxes. I would love for our state to focus more on supporting state farms first by helping promote local family owned farms.

I think better job opportunities would help my family and other families, especially in rural communities... I personally would benefit from more jobs I could do from home. It would... bring dignity and other positives to those who are limited in where or when they can work... So many small businesses... go under... because so many struggle to earn a living wage.

If I look at all the negative points about my situation and focus on the thought that some people are better off and earn money by doing easier things like running a YouTube channel, then it makes going to work a harder struggle... I think to be fair everyone should have the option available to them to either stay at a regular job working 40 hours a week or stay at home and find easier ways to earn from the Internet... We need lower cost rent for the housing and also... side jobs available for the homeless... Why should we be stuck having the only place for entertainment just going to the casinos and getting drunk...? That is just a way to set everyone up for failure.

Money is extremely tight and the price of living has only gone up while the wages have seemed to stagnant... Jobs have also become abundantly hard to achieve... with the rise of AI taking over jobs that were entry level, making it harder to even start a career. I could... start looking into getting a trade at Red River Tech and hopefully use that to build my career around it.

It's just so difficult to start a savings account because poor people really don't have much to work with by the time all the bills are paid and groceries are bought... There is so much debt, some of which is new debt just to survive the last few years... If that disappeared, I would have more choices at the grocery store instead of buying the cheapest crap there is.

I have thought of picking up better career opportunities or even starting my own business... but we need closer-to-home opportunities... The rising costs have made things more difficult on my family financially... Working would help me provide as well as help me feel as though I am doing something helpful.

I work 40 hours a week and make $17 an hour so I don't qualify for food stamps but I don't have a car and I can't save up to get a car because groceries are expensive... With a car, I don't have to worry about how I'm going to get to and from work... and it can be used when I'm not at work to do things like Uber or DoorDash and make extra income.

If me and my husband did not have to pay our Medicare premium... We pay over 700.00 a month! We could save more to have more financially security and wouldn't have to worry when we have to spend money on a necessity or when we have to replace something around the house.

I would like to be self-sufficient and own my own house... I would like it for people that are low income to be able to have a better chance on being able to own a home... whereas we can't because some of us are on a fixed income... and it's hard for us to put money aside when we can't rely on any other income during the month besides what we get on the first.

I would hope for cheaper medical insurance for my family... that goes for all insurance, car, life, homeowners... I try to save more money by cutting back on eating out, entertainment, etc. to be a little more conservative with my spending so that would help take a burden off all the high cost of living.

I hope we have financial freedom... but when inflation goes up, income never keeps up with it. We are forced to spend more on the same amount of things... If every family had enough, people could participate in more community events... and have a stronger connection to the community.

It's not the job; being with my girlfriend is my happiness, but working fast food doesn't pay for anything and we want to live alone together... Make minimum wage a little bit higher so we can actually live in this economy. It's horrible trying to live on minimum wage.

I think a livable salary so I could save money is currently outside of my control... An increased income would allow me to save for a home and save for a retirement... The cost of rental property in Oklahoma County is extremely high. As a single mom, I have to take into consideration... affordable housing.

I hope for the ability to comfortably retire... Saving and building for retirement hasn't been easy and I've stopped saving because now I can't save because of inflation... I don't buy anything in the meat department anymore because it's extremely expensive.

I would like to see the neighborhoods get fixed up and the streets get worked on and ... that people are able to afford their homes. This comes from my growing up in a community that no one cared for and it looked horrible for years because there was no one giving back to the community.

Financial stability would definitely help fast forward the progress I'm making at turtle speed right now... The price of groceries has exceeded my budget. I can't provide enough food... to sustain my family. When a person is so focused on their next meal, they are not able to thrive. That person is stuck in survival mode.

I'm going to get a second job to save money... I will do everything I can do. It is so hard... Raising the minimum wage would help me. It being so low makes it hard... Having more dollars left over after paying bills would make saving goals more achievable.

I would like to own my own home, but that’s very hard for me to do at this point. ... Many jobs in the area I live only offer minimum wage and you can’t live on that. ... I can’t do many of the jobs due to my disabilities, and driving to jobs that offer better pay isn’t a possibility for me due to having narcolepsy and proper medication is hard to come by due to my insurance which is SoonerSelect... it doesn’t allow me to see a sleep specialist because it doesn’t cover that type of specialist. ... I would love nothing more than to get my children a place to call home.

I don't currently have any money in savings. Educational professions don't get paid anything in Oklahoma... This includes support staff... I know for a fact that most of them get paid once a month and it's under $2,000. You can't live on that.

My husband and I are really good at refurbishing antiques,... but the market is not very good here. People around here don't make much money... We live in a rural area where working at a convenience store or dollar general is among the best job around here.

Poor credit has been a thorn in my husband and my sides... living paycheck to paycheck. We've never been able to get a head... we haven't had an increase in wages while cost of living has become almost undoable... If things changed, we could possibly live the American dream.

30-40-50 years ago... making 40K was decent; now it's barely scraping by. Wages have not kept up with inflation at all. When I was a kid, we struggled, but my mom was able to provide for me and 5 sisters. That's impossible now. The "traditional" family with a stay at home mom is impossible financially... Anyone not completely blind or removed can see the middle class is definitely dying.

To have a home of my own and not have to struggle with finances... The only way I know to make it happen is to keep trying to sell my baked goods and find better paying jobs... If I had the financial help then I could go back to school for baking... We need higher paying jobs. More jobs in McAlester.

I hope in 5 or 10 years I will be purchasing a house for my family. I can look for a remote company hiring for better pay than what is offered in Oklahoma... Raising the minimum wage from 7.25... would make it easier to achieve financial stability and purchase a home.

I can do anything I put my mind to... but my paychecks are keeping my money tight and I am disabled which will make it hard to find work. More companies need job opportunities for disabled people... I know too many people myself included that are one paycheck away from being homeless.

I am a skilled and certified commercial truck driver. This skill could help provide for my family if I could find a local job... A higher income would allow me to go grocery shopping and actually buy the food we want to eat instead of having to watch what we spend... If wages were increased... it would allow us to actually stop stressing about how much money we have every single day.

What makes things a little harder is the prices of groceries, gas and other things... I would like to see more big projects and events because it can help the state... get more attention and get more support. It can give people something to look forward to and have a good time instead of thinking about work.

I didn't have a career and so I have to create my own retirement fund. It's costly but I'd like to live comfortably when I'm older... It just seems that the price of living has gone up so much... We need the cost of living to come down.

As a Senior Citizen, it makes it hard... A raise in my retirement check with the State of Oklahoma would be of great assistance. It would assist in making it easier to pay monthly bills and maybe have some money left for things.

Over the past 10 years, rent has skyrocketed and sadly, wages have not. The minimum wage for the state of Oklahoma is $7.25 and has not changed in over a decade.... For example, renting a $1,000/mo apt... has an income requirement of 3x rent. You have to make $17/hr to qualify

I hope my 3 grandchildren (21-24) will be settled in a stable job... but the high interest rates for home loans... and the amount of down payment required are examples of things that hamper people just entering the work force from home ownership.

In 5-10 years I would hope that my family will have more money... the way Oklahoma is built is very anti homelessness, making it very hard to get transportation to work... Raising the minimum wage to at least $15 would help everyone be able to stay off the street... and not be stuck.

I hope 5-10 years down the line, my family will be financially free and owning multiple businesses... There have been times where people have stayed home because gas prices were too high... I've also had times where I didn't know when/if I was going to eat certain days.
I have been targeted at many different companies and I worked hard at those places... Better job security, there is none here! Trust needs to be established with good employees.

I have already gotten my Masters degree to better support my family... I may have to look for a different job outside of the teaching field to help support my family. There are less and less adults wanting to become a teacher because of lack of support and funding... It is leading to a teacher shortage.

I am 62. I would love to retire and actually enjoy the rest of my life with my husband without having to worry where the next meal comes from and how to pay the bills... I am working to make money online. My goal right now is to earn $100 a day with online sites and tasks.

We need higher wages to keep people able to afford what they need... so I'm not seeing people in my community struggle and have to decide between feeding their family or paying rent or even affording a car. I see a lot of ones in my area struggling to do all of it.

It's harder because we don't have money financially but I have a better mindset... life has to get on track sooner than later. I work on me and then I can work and help my kids and get our lives better and more on track.

My dream may be simple but it's a difficult world... Having a busy family and work life... keeps you just barley making it by... The market today is very expensive yet these regular jobs aren't paying enough to provide you the necessary resources to accomplish everything.

I am 65 years old and currently live with and take care of my 86 year old Father... I am on a fixed income, so... a cost of living raise every once in a while would be nice... as any income that could be increased would help with the cost of living. Most things are going up in prices.

I would definitely want to be in a better position financially. I would want to own a home and have a great job... I feel there is really nothing that is out of my control... But we need to make living affordable again. These high prices for houses and apartments are ridiculous... Then people would be able to afford to live on their own, without any help... and wouldn't have to choose between taking care of themselves or their children or to pay bills.

There are so many families struggling and trying to make ends meet that just want to put food on the table... With monthly support, it will be easier to breathe and live... you can afford the neccesities and the wants like celebrating kids' birthdays or holiday gifts.

I hope for the economy to be more stable, so people actually only have to work one job. The stress of cost can take a toll on you mentally and physically... I've been living in chaos and survival and I'm tired.

The debt I have accumulated... stands in the way of... shoring up all the financial issues that need attention... Chronic diseases are very difficult to manage for people with limited resources... simple issues of transportation and affording groceries are very very concerning as you age with limited income.

My wife and I both work, but we're not even really afloat right now. ... A higher wage... to match the fact the cost of living is getting higher. Having EBT reinstated would help save money up, but we make too much.

I do not make enough money and I live paycheck to paycheck which has allowed me no extra money to put towards savings. I have adult children that need my help occasionally and I am unable to help financially in any way... Providing financial support for my children in times of need would help me feel more secure as an adult as well as a more fulfilling life as a mother.

None of my friends are able to get jobs; I was lucky enough to be able to work for my mom... The jobs will post listings, but never follow up with a real interview or position. They want to work, just are not given opportunities.

I would like to see life more affordable like housing, food and healthcare... so we can provide communities that build on families who can afford to live, stay and participate without working and living check to check. It will build our communities and add to the growth of our state.

The cost of items right now is something that I cannot control. If prices went down, I could pay down debt more aggressively... to relieve stress and allow me to be happier in life... and improve the future for our family.

I hope that our families in the future will not feel like they are financially struggling. Many people our age and younger are barely able to afford necessities, let alone purchasing a house... No matter what happens in the political atmosphere, all I can do is make sure that my family's basic needs are being met.
I want to own a farm and live off grid... but it's hard right now with the price of everything still raising. I try to save then everything goes up more or again.

I'd like to be doing something I enjoy and not have to live paycheck to paycheck. ... I'm limited to what I can do now and still provide for my family. ... I wish the community had free resources that would guide me to a better career. If the workplace would invest in training that would help me succeed.

It’s difficult to move towards retirement when medical costs are so high... My doctors have prescribed other medications, ones that can make a real difference in my health, that the insurance refuses to pay for. As a result, I am not taking that medication.

I know I'm strong enough to work multiple jobs to get it done... but raising minimum wage and building more affordable housing will definitely build a better tomorrow. That would make my goals easier to achieve.

I'm hoping we can become more financially stable... I'm finding a second job or side hustle, one that can feasibly be done around three kids with a wife who also works and sharing one car... The economy getting better, a raise, or just grocery prices lowering, would help alot.

I would love to be out of some of this debt... we are selling some puppies that will help us get out of a big chunk of debt... a side effect of my wife being in so much pain is tons and tons and thousands and thousands of dollars in doctors bills. Every two weeks I pay $670.94 for horrible medical insurance and still have thousands and thousands of dollars out of our pockets that needs to be fixed!

It’s so hard for a family not on welfare to provide food without letting something else go... I see a lot of elderly who are still working at age 80 just to make ends meet. They should not be working; they should be at home gardening and cooking and relaxing.

I currently work at Sonic. I don’t make enough nor do I get enough hours. The issue with getting a new job is the market right now, nowhere will hire me yet everywhere says they’re hiring... AI being used is a big factor. There is only one day of the week I can’t work and AI rules me out as an unworthy candidate.

The state needs an economic push to get better jobs so people can then spend on things to increase the economy... I would like to see local stores busy and more homes being built that are affordable for the common person.

In 5 to 10 years I'd love to have a family and a home to raise my kids... but day to day expenses like gas, groceries and everything else have skyrocketed... 100 dollars is barely 3 shopping bags at the store.

If the cost of things would drop... I would have less stress and worry, and... more time to focus on greater things for me like going back to school to progress a career... Our goal is to grow and progress. Not make things hard for the people who keep us going.

In 5-10 years I hope to be financially set for life and be able to support my family... but something that could help me achieve a better tomorrow that is out of my control is the economy because almost everything has increased in prices... If there was a shift in the cost of living and it decreased, I would feel much more financially free and stable and be able to spend more money on other needs like groceries.

I have a small jewelry business that I love. The economy would have to get better for it to be successful again... so people could afford to purchase some things that they like or want instead of just getting by barely able to afford only what they need. They would be able to enjoy their lives instead of being stressed out all of the time just to pay their bills.

I would like... more wages so that I would have time to study for a new skill... because in Oklahoma City, public transportation has to be put to the forefront. The inability to get to jobs greatly hinders a lot of people.

It's about having the finances to be able to make it through a 24 hour period of time not stressing over if I buy something what am I going to do without to buy it, what bill isn't going to get paid in full... I'm afraid that the cost to live will be so high that my grandchildren won't even be able to think about furthering education.

I would like to have more financial freedom and be able to save and invest more instead of living paycheck to paycheck... I am a fast learner that is willing to take on more responsibility... I find ways to complete tasks instead of looking for reasons I can't do something.

I hope my family achieves financial security... but rising costs... or economic uncertainty could make achieving it harder. ...More childcare and flexible work would reduce stress, increase confidence, and allow our family to feel optimistic and secure about the future.

We really need more good paying places of employment for people of all ages and abilities, not just fast food, disability workers or people trying to earn money by selling online. ... For people to have a job that would enable them to take care of their family would be a major boost to their self confidence. I would like to see more manufacturing types of work, transportation and help for small businesses.

I am increasingly concerned about the lack of affordable housing for teachers and other service oriented occupations... More affordable housing would lead to higher quality education applicants in addition to more people going into those educational and service fields.

I hope to own my own business... I can try to get a better job, but a lot of places are not really hiring at the moment... Most families are stuck working more hours to pay for utilities, groceries, and other necessities.
I need a better paying job. ... I have lots of training and experience in advocacy. I have experience working with state leaders and I'm a people person.

If we did not have to live paycheck to paycheck it would be easier when it comes to bills but the sense of peace whether you're broke or rich is within yourself. You can still be happy and have peace with nothing.

Some of us have to choose between gas for tomorrow or dinner for tonight. If we had a neighborhood Walmart we could get stuff more cheaper... and still have some left over for gas the next day.

I hope we are not in deep poverty... The cost of living is ridiculous... minimun wage... hasn't changed since 2009. I think people would have a lot less stress and a lot less to be worried about. Then energy could be focused in other areas.

I want financial stability for me and my family. Reward those who work hard... The main thing outside of my control is affordability for basically everything. Living daily is so expensive... If major changes were made... it would feel like our hard work actually makes a difference.

Getting a better job and working as much as I can... just save, save and make it happen... I have a babygirl and she deserves to have her own room again; she deserves to have her own everything again... Just help the single moms more and help the homeless more. Give more opportunities.

A good paying job and... a car that doesn't always have problems... would fix all my problems... I wouldn't have to work as much and I'd get to spend time with my family. I also need time for my 3d printing... Jobs are just not paying enough.

A better job market for myself would mean hourly wages that coincide with the living expenses to be able to still have income without stress or worry at the end of the month. It would also be with lower taxes on the income of minimum wage or less.

For me and my family to prosper... I continue to save every day so we can get to where we want to be. Cutting back is all I’ve known... I believe that getting food costs to be lower is at least the very first thing that needs to happen. It’s a basic need and it becomes problematic for the middle class.
We are way behind in salaries for all personnel... We should have good ways for all students to leave high school excited about... making this a great state to live and work in.

The economy is what myself and most everyone else worries about. Everything is too expensive and just getting worse. ... We will not be vacationing this year... Much smaller Christmas and birthdays. I am unable to save much money these days.

If I was more financially secure, I would be able to send my kids to college. I could also work at a job that was even more fulfilling - that gives back instead of worrying about making money to pay bills.

I feel that Oklahoma’s a great place to live, but... our salaries here are not adequate for living... comfortably without struggling. ... I could use my previous skills... to go back to work, although I have retired over the last couple of years.

I’m hopeful to be debt free, own my home and have a newer car... I should also start cooking at home more... but I’m so busy, I’m not sure how I’ll achieve that... Having a higher wage would help me fix my credit, and lower rates would help me afford a home, and car.

I’m such a hard worker but I’m not seeing the fruit of my labor enough to be comfortable.

I start a new job this week, hopefully that is the first stepping stone in what I hope to be a pathway to stability... The economy for the average American is in freefall yet corporations are raking in record profits... this country needs to take real efforts to break up monopolies and curb anti-competitive behavior.

Being able to afford rent and utilities is almost impossible on $10 an hour... Yes, many teens work in fast food as a first time job but many adults are forced to work these jobs because it’s all they can get, and that leads to an increase in the poverty levels among the state of Oklahoma.

What I would like to see in my community is more of a job opportunity... There really isn't anything around here but fast food... If there were better jobs, people would work, and there wouldn't be so much poverty and struggle in the community.
The amount of debt and bills we have make it very tough with only one of us working... The country is very hard to survive in with a family because of having to go without.

I want to invest my money in good stocks or in some business with which I can live on the profits... but the nation's economy is out of my control... its instability is currently one of the factors that our money does not reach as much as before.

One thing I hope that will be true in 5 to 10 years is inflation going down and prices of everything going down... I would like to see the homeless helped out since I see them on the side of the road every time I’m driving and gas prices a little bit lower.

I'm not looking to get rich, I just want to be able to live the rest of my life comfortably. Right now I'm in debt and can't afford to keep standard things going, such as keeping warm or fixing everything that this house needs.

My hope is to be more financially secure. I am trying to make that happen with a second job... but there are several things that could make my tomorrow better that are outside of my control... Improving education will improve many other aspects of the community.

I hope that we own our home and/or vehicle and have 6-12 months of savings built up... This would be possible with lower costs in grocery, housing and energy prices paired with increased access to low interest loans.

I hope for things like paying off my mortgage, adding to my nest egg, and increasing retirement cash... but Seniors get hit triple by inflation, increased taxes, and increased instability for Social Security which makes it more difficult.

My hope is making $25.00 a hour... We need to vote in the people to write new laws into the state constitution for labor laws... like raising minimum wages for all and lowering grocery prices.

I hope my family can come to financial stability and stop having to waste their lives slaving away into dead end jobs living paycheck to paycheck. ... More opportunities to learn better skills and trades would be extremely beneficial for myself and others in my area.

It's like spending money is my happy place sometimes or getting my girls stuff... I would like to see... a decent park to take our kids to play... especially for the families that can’t afford to take their kids anywhere.

We should be Oklahoma strong but I don’t see y’all trying to figure out what your going to cook for the week or how long can you push the light bill so you can go to the doctor.

The contrast between scrambling alone versus being backed by predictable systems is what shaped the belief that income stability and community support matter most.

I hope for a future where everyone haves a great income and medical insurance to be able to afford housing... Because too many people are poor and can't get proper help with being sick.

I’d like the state to raise the minimum wage... It would give more stability and security. Everything is increasing but our wages. People are one paycheck away from being homeless.

I hope to make sure that my family members are safe and well off financially... by finding a better job, so I can financially help myself with what ever I need help with in my life.

I will own my own home and I plan on making this truth by... working 60-80 hour weeks for the next 6 years... With financial support, I could immediately put those close to me in new and safer places to live and better schools

Paying off my collections would help me achieve a better tomorrow... since I’m not being paid what I’m worth. I would then be able to apply for apartments and move out of my childhood home.

I wish we can make this world... where people don't have to struggle to make a living... I know it's hard in this world to make it, but if we all lean together we can make it.

I would hope that we would be in a positive place financially... enough to be able to help others in need... to go to the grocery store and be able to buy not only the essentials that we need but also extra for others.

Knowing that my family is financially secure would help me a great deal and I myself wouldn’t have to work so much to just make ends were met. I would like to be able to own a house... I try to save money, but everything is so much more expensive such as utilities, food and interest rates... I’m afraid it will only get worse and put us in a bad place and dash our hopes.

I hope that I have my dream job acquired... A better job would make things a lot easier... For the homeless community, they need... some forms of jobs to earn their self respect back... Without self respect you have nothing.

My ultimate hope is that inflation balances itself out and people can get a chance at living a fair shot at life... My hope is that the housing market becomes more affordable so that families can get in a neighborhood and form communities within.

To be financially stable... a new job and a new house... I would like to see us be able to have more shopping centers like other clothing stores and different fast food restaurants that other states have.
I would like to see... more and better jobs! And up our pay just like every other state... This is coming from a citizen born and raised here.

We need wages that will actually help... get through every day-to-day life, because inflation is taking over. ... I cannot keep killing myself and still not making it… This state desperately needs to create more jobs with livable wages or more opportunities for those of us that barely make enough to eat but too much for any assistance. We are not asking for handouts, just a way to make it in the world.
The cost of living... makes it hard for the average person to be able to save money. Most companies do the average 3% raise when the cost of living goes up about 5%.

In 5 to 10 years I hope that I will have a better career that pays well enough to make me feel comfortable... I would like to see the inflation reduce more so people can afford the necessities without feeling stressed.

I would hope everything gets more affordable and that life can be easier for everyone... We need housing support and employment support for sure. The job market seems rough at the moment and should be more accessible.
I hope to be able to have our house paid off and be able to have money saved... but spending what money I could save on medication is the reason it is hard to save.

We need more higher paying jobs for people with felonies. Where we can get ahead.

My copayments for medicine and doctor visits take about a third of my monthly income. I make just over the cutoff line for State aid... and grocery prices themselves have risen so dramatically that I cannot afford even the cheapest meats. Thank goodness for food banks and my children who help.

I work 2.5 jobs as a single mom... my health is poor and I don't know how much longer I can keep going... It feels the government wants to keep you trapped in this cycle. For example, if I work X amount of overtime or get a $1.00 raise then the little assistance I do get, gets lowered or cut off... We need to change the requirements... so that getting raises and/or overtime will not affect your assistance so drastically and you can actually climb out of the hole and actually break the cycle and succeed. 90% of the people on assistance, do NOT want to be on assistance. We just want to take care of our families and live...not survive, LIVE.

I was very self-sufficient... I had my own company before and I've always worked and provided for my children... My health has in the past few years went down extremely... It seems like disability is extremely hard to get approved... and there is a stigma in Oklahoma that when you file a disability you're automatically going to get disapproved... people that need help will... just put it off because they think it won't happen.

I would hope to be free of excessive expenses that have made it impossible to have any savings for emergencies. As a person on a fixed income due to being on disability, it is nearly impossible to increase my income... I recently had to replace the tires on my vehicle as they were literally worn to the point they would not hold air. I had to borrow money from a relative to be able to get new ones, and then had to sell personal items to pay them back.

We just want to have an enjoyable life not marked by the constant thoughts of whether or not we'll have enough to pay our bills... There is assistance, but we're past the threshold. Even though we're past it, it's still hard living in a 5 person family. What we earn just isn't enough even though we're "stable" financially.

I work 40 hours a week and make $17 an hour so I don't qualify for food stamps but I don't have a car and I can't save up to get a car because groceries are expensive... With a car, I don't have to worry about how I'm going to get to and from work... and it can be used when I'm not at work to do things like Uber or DoorDash and make extra income.

I would change the income guidelines... They do not take into consideration inflated prices at the store as well as the amount we pay for utilities... or how much is deducted from my paycheck for health insurance... I pay $400 a month for my insurances... I pay $1065 for mortgage and $400 for a vehicle... so people like me can’t buy much groceries... I would like to see more middle class workers receive help... If the government officials were given the amount of money I have left over after bills to feed their family for two weeks maybe it would help them to understand.

I would be able to start a business that brings in money and I'd be able to save us ... but I've asked for assistance for years and kept being told I made $20 too much. I understand limitations but it really is hard.

My wife and I both work, but we're not even really afloat right now. ... A higher wage... to match the fact the cost of living is getting higher. Having EBT reinstated would help save money up, but we make too much.

We make more on one income to qualify for help, but we struggle... Changing the income thresholds... could really impact my family... Life in general, would be much more affordable and attainable. Less stress on the adults and would allow us to be more present with kids.

I started working at 14 and worked most of my life and raised 4 children by myself. When I had to retire due to cancer, I had to move in with one of my children because I lost my house and donâ€™t make enough to live alone... My child barely makes it and because I live with him there is help I donâ€™t qualify for. So there is always living from check to check and no one gets ahead.

I’m a single mom working full time but I can’t qualify for food stamps... I make $15/hour and have 2 teenagers. We can’t afford food... Our local grocery store is so much higher than going out of town... It’s almost as though the theme of the grocery store is “keep the poor, poor”. It costs less to eat fast food than to buy groceries for one meal for my family.

The nation's social security infrastructure is crumbled. The lack of employees makes it harder to get your benefits, that is if you get them at all before you die... people are dying while they are rejected for the social security not one, not two, maybe three times or more and being put on hold for hours having to get lawyers. It's ridiculous.

I cannot keep killing myself and still not making it… This state desperately needs to create more jobs with livable wages or more opportunities for those of us that barely make enough to eat but too much for any assistance. We are not asking for handouts, just a way to make it in the world.
I am told I get too much money for any assistance... I'm unable to work because of the pain I’m in all the time.

Making more money to help me achieve a better tomorrow because then I could move towards my dream house. ... Adults have a hard time... when they don’t have health insurance because they make too much. It shouldn’t matter if you make too much; they should still qualify for health insurance.

I would like to see people come together instead of each race staying within their own communities. We could all enjoy each other's diversities and culture if there was a way to bridge the gap. ... I've met every race while giving rides and all are good friendly people. But that is one on one. Imagine if we all came together, amazing it would be.

I have been a joyful advocate for Oklahoma since I've lived here and am dedicated to helping the state move forward... If we trust all residents may elevate our state and cherish residents, then we have more individuals on board to thwart any meanies interrupting tranquil Oklahoma.

As a community, there is not as much of a focus on community as I'd expect from such a Christian state. I think the world is better when we care about the well-being of our neighbors, but there is very much a 'us vs them' mentality that seems to drive decisions from the personal to the state level.

Recognize that differences make us stronger... I know many immigrants who are hard-working and no different from those of us born in the US. They should not be stereotyped or demonized. Education on this and other topics is key... we see the world is NOT black and white. There are infinite perspectives, and no one person or group has all the answers.
I would vote for someone that doesn't target different ethnicities... I try to participate in community events and show love to all of my neighbors no matter they're ethnicity.

I do desire... that the political differences not be so divisive... for all different views to join together for the sake of unity... But people are becoming isolated within each neighborhood... I hope that we will live in a world with no racism nor discrimination... I would like to see unity with all races... and everyone come together as one. I have personally experienced moments in the community where people come together at church and church events and this has shown happiness for everyone.

Finding a faith community that isn't controlling or secretive has been very difficult... Complaints from people in my community give me the impression our voices aren't being heard... We want to be able to live freely and not worry about other ideologies creeping in to make life more miserable.

Oklahoma has made huge strides in being recognized for being a great place to live, and as a state that supports each other known as the Oklahoma Standard. ... We treat humans with kindness... As a parent of Native American kids, my kids shouldnâ€™t have to carry their tribal papers so they arenâ€™t harassed in the state they were born in.
I would like to see more LGBTQ+ rights... I have someone close to me in that lifestyle and they have a very hard time in Oklahoma. They are sometimes scared to go to school and be themselves.

I wish all children grew up and not be abused or put down. I wish ladies were not treated like they are less than men... Women get paid less money for the same job. I wish DHS paid more attention when people made complaints on abused children. On the news a child was missing 10 days... The parents went to jail for abuse. Tips had been sent to them.

Stand up for the people that vote for you, do what you were elected to do, and stop making everything political, democrats vs Republicans; we are all just people trying to live, not a party. Keep trash and litter off of Oklahoma streets... and work on helping the homeless... Some of us are about 2 paychecks away from being homeless. I would like to see both parties come together and work on this and set an example.

I believe you have to follow the Golden Rule, treat others as you would have them treat you... it's hard to do that when they want to treat you bad but that's when you must show dignity and respect... I wish the government and churches would have programs that help bring people together cuz there's so many forces out there that are trying to divide us apart.
Respect my neighbors regardless of race. Help where I can. I live in a diversified neighborhood and I can tell some of them don't trust me because I am white.

Some families in my neighborhood avoid going out or speaking up because they fear it could put them at risk, even for small things like attending school meetings or community events... Policies that protect families would not only reduce fear and stress but also allow people to contribute fully to their communities... and strengthen our community as a whole by fostering trust, opportunity, and a sense of belonging for everyone.

It appears that radical groups are better protected than the ordinary person. I've been accosted by homeless while trying to do grocery shopping, or people attempting to get in your face to yell or scream insults... with no protection... I just believe some type of boundaries should be put in place keeping these people accountable for their actions.

Many places hire ONLY family and friends so it’s next to impossible to get a job if you are not part of the ‘in crowd’. ... My granddaughter was working at a local Bath and Bodyworks, and after it moved they kept the ones related to and friends with the longer time employees and just quit scheduling the others to work.

Having a friendship with our neighborhood pastors has given me insight into action that can be taken. Small steps lead to big action... Letting children have equal opportunity, regardless of background... paves the way for a stronger community.

I think there should be less of an us versus them mentality and more of how can we work together to help others live more successful lives. ... I have had very traumatic experiences growing up... I have seen so many sides of life. People are so much more than the circumstance they are in at that moment. Small acts of kindness can go so far and usually doesnâ€™t cost a thing.

I know this isn't the good life my grandparents always talked about.... I hope leaders will focus more on making our home, Oklahoma, feel like a home again... by getting us back to spending time with our family.... The good life is being able to work a simple 9 to 5 job... and come home with no worry in the world... and no stress from over working because you can't make the bills or being divided because some popularity, childish campaign is going on. Our leaders doing things for country and people.
My old home town was very pro immigrant and helped the community and its people feel safe.

By talking to my neighbors, we all would feel more of a combined neighborhood... It would help us feel like we are appreciated and that all citizens are equally important. There is definitely a division in how various neighborhoods are treated.

I live on tribal land but do not have any rights as a tribal citizen due to my family’s lack of records... I would have access to tribal inclusion and feel as if I belonged to my tribe. It would also open doors to opportunities that my children and I have not had available to us in the past.

I don’t think many people understand how to be open minded and tolerant of each other... For some of my friends they cannot reveal their political beliefs in fear of retaliation. I can’t even wear a California sweatshirt without a rude comment from someone at my doctor's office! I wish our leaders would calm the nation instead of agitating everyone.

Teamwork at home like in the workplace makes it easier to deal with on the daily... and could build relationships and provide a sense of achievement and a belonging to the community rather than just a person receiving help. People need a sense of belonging for there to be an active change.

I would say just the general idea of just being a team... I would like for our community and states to get along better, help each other out when others are suffering. Just because we have it don't mean everyone else does... to teach the new generation to share and respect other wishes.

I wish crime rates were a lot lower here... if everyone learned equality, then we can achieve that. I think if we had more parades or events going on in the city where everyone can come together, then that'll bring a bit more peaceful interactions.

I would love to be a part of a community task force that talks to people and finds out what could help them live better more fulfilling lives... I believe that people have lost some trust in government leaders. I think a community task force of neighbors or regular people facing similar problems would get more buy in and trust from Oklahomans.

There seems to be a disconnect with the younger generations as advice from those who came before falls on deaf ears. Respect for our elders... is slowly eroding... We should bring in the retired elders to share their stories of overcoming life's struggles, finding common sense solutions and learning from past mistakes to avoid making them again.

After seeing the recent murder of Renee Good, and all of the examples of peaceful protests turned violent due to ICE officers becoming violent... I would enjoy to see a more connected state. We must remain positive and never spread the hate to others no matter what.

I'd like to see basic human empathy... protecting the land, the water, the community. Not making everything about politics and money... I have my moments where I... try to build something towards helping my community but sadly nobody cares unless you have money or drugs.

It starts with me and my family... I started a nonprofit to help with community safety... We need compassion for the forgotten and overlooked... and better representation of everyone, not just the elite and rich.

It's hard to find the right balance. You can be a nice person with a big heart, but people like to push down those people and hurt their feelings... A leader should work with people and help them grow together. To be a good leader you need to respect and love your people.

I would like to see our state representatives, our governor, and our senators come together as a team. Plain and simple. Team members work towards the betterment of all parties involved...
