The good of the community has been lost by those who swore to uphold it and support it.

When we are acquainted with others, there is a better chance of good relations & working to improve life for all residents... It is really sad when someone you know has no info or knowledge of matters that affect all of us. Pay attention-it could save or make life better.

What I would really like to see is people on both sides learning to live with each other and allow each other space and freedom to be themselves as long as they don't hurt anyone. ... I just wish our elected representatives saw us all as human beings, rather than pawns or obstacles to be removed. ... If you view someone else as a human being, you're less likely to commit violence against them, and probably more likely to work out some sort of compromise, rather than try to eliminate them.

I hope we can make America great again like helping everyone get along with each other and be happy... We could spread kindness to one another and offer neighbors to go to church with you or help with food or gas or anything... But if someone needs a ride, I personally can’t because I don’t drive.

I want less fighting, less crime, less killing... There has to be a law... to keep Christians safer, especially pastors that go out and preach the word of God. Because Charlie wasn't doing anything wrong. All he did was share his faith... and for that he was murdered and now his wife and his three children have to go on without a husband and Dad. It doesn't seem fair.

I hope we can get to a point that we are less divided... by being willing to hear other sides, be more open and understanding of other viewpoints and find common ground... We, both democrat and republican constituents, want you to listen, really listen to us.

I think that... we want everybody to get along... getting along and agreeing are not the same. We need to be united because... we ultimately want everybody to be the best they can be... until somebody says, 'Hey, I'm going to act in love instead of what I want,'... it's not going to change... I'm not going to try to control this situation; I'm going to help guide it... if we're all just taking up slack and... nobody's trying to be... the head honcho, then we can all work together.

Stop the racism... We should definitely take care of our own people, no matter what their circumstances are... There are deaths, shootings in my neighborhood. Lots of fights in violence. Really sad and scary.

I would like the political divide to be closed... Do not automatically dismiss Democratic (or even more liberal) ideas because they are not Republican ideas. DEI is a great beginning to help show that others matter.

In my community, I wish it was not just the same group of people who have a say... about everything. The community as a whole is not represented... Let people speak without being made to feel stupid... really listening, not just nodding and moving on with their own agenda.

I would like to see everyone get the same opportunity to become better rather than just the same group... I live in south Okc and we don’t have as much opportunity... as someone who lives in Nichols Hills.
It would be easier for us to work together as a whole as a community and teach our kids how to work together with each other's businesses.
I know it's hard in this world to make it, but if we all lean together we can make it... We need to help each other. Help clean up our city.

The workforce wants younger people to hire because they are the future, yet this younger generation has no desire to work and stay with a company. In the meantime, your older generation like me who gives 200% to their job, is not looked at because we are too old… Older workers have been through the trials and tribulations of working for the past 30 to 40 years. We have the knowledge that it takes to succeed. If these younger generations could… shadow one of the older workers… it could make a huge difference in… their work ethics.

I love living in Oklahoma. We moved here from Minnesota. I really want Oklahoma to stay Oklahoma and not become Minnesota... we fled Minnesota where we were being taken over by Islam. They took over our neighborhoods and our schools... If we don’t put laws through now heading this off, they’ll come here and infiltrate our cities and it’ll be too late then.

I want to see everyone come together... People have forgotten that their political leanings are not their entire identity. People are less concerned with helping others and more concerned about blaming those that need help... Who cares if someone buys their child a birthday cake with their food stamps? I couldnâ€™t deny that to someone just because it came from the government.

I would hope state leaders would take into account our Native population and their needs here... It would make relationships between native governments and state and local governments run smoothly. Much more could be accomplished for the good of everyone.
I am very active in my community. We look out for each other... I’m transgender and living here is frankly miserable... I would feel safer being myself out in public and with my friends.

I had a phone interview for a job where the interviewer thought I am white from my voice sound. When she met me in person for the interview she said I do not look anything like I sound. Needless to say, I did not get the job.

I would hope that the racism here in Oklahoma ends. We need to all be one unite. We deal with it here just stepping out our door... It’s up to the people to change. We can only educate them, help them see everyone is human.

My family raised me to love on everyone and be a supporter to all, we are all God's children... But I see their language, their bullying of people... everything is all hate, nothing happiness or love.
I want better communication with everyone that’s in our circle and/or community... and I’d like to see more involved people as a whole coming together for the better future we strive to be a part of.

We as humans need to work together and create a shared effort to strive for greatness... I would love to see strengthened connections with tribal leaders while also respecting their cultures, values and communities... you can have your own opinions and beliefs but the basics of facilitating discussion and finding a common group is respect.

What would help me... is a better support system from family and Friends... to know that you have someone on your side no matter what... I would like to see families coming together... I just feel like we all should become one.

A little empathy goes a long way, but we need to invest in the children because they are the future of this country. ... With things like the death of Nex Benedict, school shootings, and bullying, these things are so crazy that happened and yet they want to ban books from the library?

I am over the hate-filled Republican and Democratic parties... I believe on a lot more issues we'd all actually agree on. But nobody focuses on that. It's all about allegiance to the left or the right... Wrong is wrong, moral is moral, no matter your party affiliation. And if you choose to be ignorant, turn a blind eye, and protect others solely based on if they are left or right, you gotta go because WE the people are DONE.

I have tried every LOGICAL thing to inform and educate... the only outcome is that I have to walk away. For my own sanity, and sometimes safety... I would love to see Oklahoma leaders quit trying to gain the favor of lunatics, and stand up for their fellow Oklahomans and do what is in the best interest of the people, as a whole.

Fairness and equality for every race... I feel that if a person needs help, physically or mentally, there should be a helping hand there. Not if you qualify for this and that... there are still honest people who need the assistance.

I would like to see racism end and bring along all skin tones to like one another without so much politics in the way... It’s sad seeing people get separated from their families and loved ones due to politics.
Right now I feel like we are too divided... you could start by actually hearing people out and not s---ting them down immediately. Actually caring for others and not everyone for themselves.

I want to make sure that people have the same care as everyone else. ... People with Medicare or commercial insurance are... treated better than the people on Medicaid because it’s for people that’s on low income.

I dream of the day when people are proud of their country again and can focus on real world problems instead of being crutched by woke rhetoric... The American Dream was based upon these rights... you cannot have the American Dream without those important rights... I want to be proud to go out and say I’m an Oklahoman living the American Dream.

I wish I had received the love and attention I should’ve gotten as a child... I would like to see honestly everyone coming together as one/ a whole and doing better for the kids in the community around us.
I would like to see our whole state work together better as a whole... I feel like there would be amazing changes and we would actually get things accomplished and get them accomplished a lot sooner.

I would like to see a return of open dialogue... We need to work towards goals that benefit everyone without intentionally marginalizing other groups for simply having differing values... When groups of people feel marginalized, it is a natural reaction to lash out at everything.
Feeling accepted makes things a lot easier. ... I just want to see people treated better and people being genuinely nice to each other for no reason.

I would hope for my family that we would live somewhere that makes laws based on what is best for average people... but instead, increased violence, increased hatred, and vitriol are evident in everyday occurrences.
I'm black, I live in a white state that doesn't care about me... State leaders ain't doing nothing for ME. And my kind... I am black and I need more representation.
I would like to see more togetherness... every day life as a whole feels like there is less and less inter-family human communication or contact.

I would like to see more understanding towards people and less violence... The most important change comes from helping others feel like they belong and that they are apart of this community despite having differences.
I hope I finally get accepted as a trans woman... I would like to see anti trans laws get abolished... because hatefulness is wrong.

I would like to see... a renewed sense of national pride and trying to work together... For example, the fact that congressmen can get rich off their position incentives people looking for financial gain, rather than their constituents.
I hope to be living in a loving accepting Oklahoma... I think everyone should be welcome here whether ‘illegal’ or not... I support people that come here just wanting better for their families.

I hope that this transgender nonsense is done and over with... I don't think there is much I can do to help stop that from continuing... I would love for the little people's voices to be heard like me.

I'd like to see everyone come together and stop the stupid stuff that's been going on around here... so everything will be peaceful and calm and the town would be so much better without all the violence and nonsense.

We need a better definition for tolerance. It is all one sided. In trying to defend one, the rest of us are being disrespectful... We need to have the right to stand up for ourselves. We have lost that with the tolerance movement.
I hope that everyone learns to just get along and find peace within their own life... I will stand up for whomever needs it as long as it is right.
I would love if more people came together with and for each other, to treat and care for each other as equals and not segregated from rich to poor.
It don't matter how hard you work. If you have even been in trouble or have a record, they won’t give you the time of day. People change.
We need people coming together to get stuff done instead of working against one another... We could start by having small get togethers, like fundraisers... for bettering the community we all share.

It would help by getting the community on a local level to agree and believe in one belief system so that we can make things easier... We need just something that we can all at the community get behind and believe in.

State leaders must always be representing we voters as though we are their family and what they pass is something they will never feel hurts us, their family... that in the end, it grows the state in all areas, for the betterment of all people.

I would like to see the judicial system to be looked at. ... Follow the law as it is written. Not look and say your parents are good people so you get a slap on the wrist while another person is charged with the same things and because his family is a nobody, you are going to jail for a long time. Not fair.
People need to work together as a one... Helping your neighbors out when they need it. Doing things for them without asking.

It would be better by bringing us together and helping each other instead of being against each other, being filled with hate instead of love... People have no empathy anymore and don’t seem to care... if they put themselves in their shoes, they would get it and understand that some people just need some help.
People as a whole have seemed divided recently by all sorts of topics... Regardless of leaders in charge, a community can still work on ensuring the topics and atmosphere of their own community.
To bond community and neighborhoods together... The snobs pretend they get served rather than participating in the help of caring for their neighbors.

A better tomorrow would be for not so much hate between Republicans and Democrats. The name calling is out of control.... People are so rigid in their ideas they won’t listen to opposing ideas. Schools should teach how to have civil conversations.
Communities need to come together... to better things within our communities and be able to spread them state wide.
Civil issues like racism and inequality being solved would make me happy... and I would like to see more funding being provided for our education system.
I would like it if equality was shown more... Everyone would have a voice in decisions. The community would come together and discuss to find an equally beneficial solution.
I hope for freedom of religion, race, sexuality and the core democratic values that our so-called leaders don’t adhere to now... Our freedoms are no longer guaranteed and America is not great.
If I can, they can... People need to be learning what respect means... No one is any better than the other.

Racism, xenophobia, and other-izing rip apart the ability for a community to really come together... If certain groups of society feel unwelcome, unhelped, or discouraged, then the whole cannot be whole. Society will remain fractured.

I would like to see our State leaders... place high priority on communicating in a manner that sets an example of what it means to truly “love your neighbors” no matter their race, gender, or political party... and refrain from using any harsh, demeaning, preferential, or unkind language, but rather lead the citizens of Oklahoma in expressing how to treat one another
We can live life with not being paranoid, kids can be kids and play outside... just keep us safe and help us get better job opportunities for us Black men... give us a chance.

There is a general expectation from certain individuals that America is one singular culture... This leads to people believing that not every person deserves the same levels of respect, resources, and support... This creates a divide and separates people.
I want people to actually get along... and not be mean.

Just that all the prejudice, racism and general disrespect for those who are lesser would stop... It would help everyone feel they are on equal footing, and that some people are not better than others.
I would like for the people to... think about something besides themselves... People are too concerned about immigration.
Leaders are always land greedy and all about money, they don't care about the community.... They don't respect indigenous people. They don't respect the culture. They don't respect the land.

If we could all just get along and stop fighting over money, why can’t we just feed each other?... Everybody’s always trying to pick sides and s---... Everybody’s forced to pick a team... What team are you on? I don’t know. What team are you on?
We need to demonstrate a positive outlook and work towards a spirit of cooperation... so that politicians are working together for better conditions for everyone.
Maybe people would come together and do more... to keep an eye out for each other.

Less stupid government propaganda and fear mongering, more American patriots... less handouts. More discipline. ... Simple, history. We didn’t win 2 world wars and a civil war by being f---s and q----s.
Leave the politicking, leave the political division between Republicans and Democrats, unite the country, unite the cities.

Affordable housing would give them a sense of belonging in the community. It would make them want to be part of community activities... I grew up in the 60s and 70s and we had neighborhood schools. Each neighborhood took pride in their school and many activities happened there.

As a member of a faith based outreach organization, we cooked and handed out food to the community... If everyone within city limits donated $1 to their city hall every month... it would drastically decrease the numbers of the hungry and homeless within our cities of Oklahoma.

Bring the old downtowns alive again instead of just empty buildings... Get volunteers... to help elderly keeping their yard up or small repairs to their homes that they can't afford to have done... It's a way to meet new people... Alot of people don't even leave their houses, especially the elderly, so they can socialize.

I would like to see us be more involved with the community and also helping the less fortunate being seen and known for us to know that they matter... It's about Thanksgiving meals and Christmas emergency packs and being out and about and not treating them like they are less than us.
Knowing you have the extra food is a comfort and you can help someone else if they need food or hungry... I feel like the food bank can be helpful but we have one that gives out old yucky food.

I hope we will live in a society that actually cares about others and helps each other not only survive, but thrive... I'm watching my brother and his wife struggling to support their kids. My sister-in-law is a teacher, and my brother is a school counselor with a masters degree, and he's having to work at Reasors part time so they can scrape by. It shouldn't be this difficult... This is not the life I was promised as a kid.

We need neighborhood meetings where everyone can air grievances or talk about how they can help their community... We step over what we don't like. We ignore what we don't want to look at and that is the mentally ill homeless people... I'm not saying I have solutions but if there was something I could do like volunteer work or help in some way, I would. I just don't have any answers.

There are a good number of local food pantries but nowhere to receive free household supplies (soap, tissue, baggies, foil, cleanser, etc.)... I can only believe it will improve our lives by making it possible to stay healthy and clean. That would make me happy.

Teaching compassion and exposure to cultures that are not our own... helps to build trust... I've seen the opposite when hearing the churches preach love and compassion and watching the pastor zoom out of the parking lot in his new sports car almost hitting a homeless lady and never stopping. True story.

I wish all children grew up and not be abused or put down. I wish ladies were not treated like they are less than men... Women get paid less money for the same job. I wish DHS paid more attention when people made complaints on abused children. On the news a child was missing 10 days... The parents went to jail for abuse. Tips had been sent to them.

Stand up for the people that vote for you, do what you were elected to do, and stop making everything political, democrats vs Republicans; we are all just people trying to live, not a party. Keep trash and litter off of Oklahoma streets... and work on helping the homeless... Some of us are about 2 paychecks away from being homeless. I would like to see both parties come together and work on this and set an example.

They are taking the road of least resistance to follow the president without any thought of what we want. They listened and said all the right things when they needed our votes but then nothing happened. Nothing moved forward for our communities and what our needs are.
I don't think state leaders care at all about the people, they just want to keep their money coming in... If they actually cared, things would be better already.

Politicians should be allowing people to live their life to the best of their ability as long as it's not hurting anyone else... We need to start holding criminals in politics to immediate consequences... It would hold them to a higher standard... and show that criminal behavior is not protected just because you're an elected official. No one is above the law.

We are 50th in education because money is not being handled properly and our children are suffering. Money from the lottery was supposed to boost our education system and money from casinos was supposed to boost our education system. Where is that money going?

It really seems like more often than not, when the government gets involved in something in order to "fix" an issue, the issue either stays the same, or gets worse... For example, trying to "fix" the housing market after the "crash" did nothing but bail out large institutions... ultimately making the American Dream of buying and owning your own house impossible for most of the population... STOP WITH THE GREED AND CORRUPTION. YOU WORK FOR THE PEOPLE... NOT JUST THE MOST WEALTHY AMONG THEM... YOU ARE KILLING AMERICA AND ANY SEMBLANCE OF THE AMERICAN DREAM.

We vote people into office to help the people of this great state of Oklahoma. But instead they help themselves... Y’all need to do better instead of building parks, fix our roads and help the people of this state... you would start to see this state thriving... the roads around here are just unacceptable. DO BETTER.
We should not let individuals get away with crime because of their family ties. Jessie Butler should never have been treated as a child and released while victims have life-long injuries.

The people in leadership of our community embezzle that money and everyone knows it... They need to realize everyone knows...what they are up to and that we are not stupid people... We need to be heard, they need to hear everyone’s story.
We need term limits on all political offices... It would take fat income and asset gain from the enticement to make politics a career and start enticing people who genuinely care to serve.
I would like more police presence and stricter enforcement. That includes enforcement against all representatives of our state.

Lawmakers should have normal jobs and live like everyone else... Term limits and not being paid year round are two things that help end the grift they have become accustomed to. Then people would be more likely to SERVE because they want to do what is best for everyone.

We need the elimination of the Oklahoma Turnpike Authority, and a thorough corruption investigation of the Oklahoma Department of Transportation. These are dependent on a Governor, Attorney General, and a legislature willing to do the right thing... Honesty and transparency in State Government... will lead to a better Oklahoma for all.
I would like to see help from the state leaders... and active actions. Meaning don’t just talk about doing something but have something to show for the words.

We need leaders that are the best for the role, not who they know and how much money they can raise... The entire political process needs to be overhauled. There are smart people who will never run for office because of the broken system.
I hope the economy would change for the better under a different leadership that is more for the people and not their pockets.
We need to find government that works for its people, not for the money... We need tomorrow's generation to succeed where we have failed.
We need a better state budget and to stop providing funding illegally to foreign immigrants... The Somalia fraud situation of billions of dollars in fraud will cause our people to be deprived.
We want to believe the government has our best interest in mind, but they don't. ... Have clarity in our government. Stop the corruption from the top and arrest those involved.

Actually do what you say you are going to do. Where do my taxes actually go? Obviously not the roads because they are still s---... Where’s all the money from medical marijuana? Obviously not in education because we’ve gone backwards.

Start cracking down on fraud, mismanagement, and corruption... We see an abuse of power and loss of money, and not having qualified people in charge of projects... Stick to what you are supposed to do and quit blaming others for your problems.
We can achieve a better tomorrow... if the politicians will do what they promise to do... Every election they promise to fix the health care problem but until now nothing happened.

It is time to get the 'good ole boys' out of our government... and get out of the way for younger, brighter minds to lead... to get big oil and big pharma out of politics and start helping the people first.

I would like a cleaner city that is not in disrepair, and better help with the housing help. When I was looking into getting housing help it took way too long... I was waiting for two years. More updates on the process for your case, more funding and other ways to help people afford their rent when they are struggling to pay would be helpful.

Citizens... don't know how to file many types of reports or violation complaints which leads to exploitation of those who actively have a voice they can't express. ... I have been trying to report my work for health code violations and my landlord for tenant right violations but the path to do so is long and murky if you can't find or afford legal representation.

I'm not going to lie, I don't fallow politics... But people out here need help, need chances to grow, jobs that understand people make mistakes. It's how you come back from those mistakes that show the person you really are... People feel they have no other choice but to make money in whatever they can... Cause it is not easy getting help from the state... if one person works... that one person makes too much by like 40 dollars to get help, so they do bad things to get what the family needs. It's a cycle, and with out better help it won't ever change... I know for a fact background check companies do not have the right and true information... There should be a time frame for some things. If some one has not been in anymore trouble... they should be able to go in to the court clerk... and get things... taken off.

I live in Tahlequah, the Cherokee Nation Capitol. I would like to see more state run programs. I am not a Native American... Almost all programs of any kind here are run by the Cherokee Nation and I do not qualify. The non-natives in this area need help too... Healthcare. They have WW Hastings hospital and all the clinics that go along with it and they pay nothing for anything. Completely free healthcare.

Something I would like to see happen in my community and my state is reinstating the ability of teachers and administrators to discipline students... My husband has worked in a middle school for the last four years and is horrified at how students are allowed to behave however they want because administration does not want to discipline them and then have to deal with parents. Right now, students are running the schools because there is no discipline.

A home ownership program that actually helps real people who have had credit challenges would be a great start. I have this discussion with my peers on a weekly basis... Current programs require more money down than many of us have saved. This should be updated to reflect our current predicament.

I would love to get a bill passed in congress to help the everyday people and stop protecting doctors... I had a doctor mess up on a surgery in 2017. I have had 16 surgeries since then... I should have sued... but there is a 2-year statute of limitations, and I spent more than 2 years trying to figure out what was wrong with me. Now I am too late... 2 years is too quick, especially when I have 6 different doctors trying to figure out what is wrong.

My wife was harassed at the courthouse when we went to get our wedding license. She used her tribal ID, which is federally recognized and yet three people told her that she couldn’t use that as her ID... The government’s actions of supporting local and national officials detaining, harassing, and harming, my fellow tribal brothers and sisters have confirmed my belief... At this point with ICE detaining tribal members I feel helpless and scared. I don’t go outside if I don’t have to.

I'd like to see it NOT BE ILLEGAL TO BE "HOMELESS." ... These new statutes making the unhoused citizens either chose JAIL or REHAB borders on some form of government that is NOT democracy. I have struggled to keep a roof my entire life and lived without one many times. It's difficult enough to survive, without laws like that.

One thing I would like to see happen in our state is the housing or HUD to open back up because I'm facing homelessness... When I was chosen to get a voucher the first time, it was a shock to me about how I have to figure out a way to come up with my deposits and application fees... When my voucher was supposed to expire, I was needing more time... but housing never gave me an extension.

It appears that radical groups are better protected than the ordinary person. I have been accosted by homeless while trying to do grocery shopping, or people attempting to get in your face to yell or scream insults... with no protection. ... Change is hard because the process to create laws or regulations takes so long and some people, like myself, are elderly and don’t have the strength or time to muddle through the red tape to create change.

I'd love to see state government to stop with partisan politics but instead invest in the community... At the local level, the ones that have tried to invest have found it difficult to work with the city. Tax demands or permitting costs have driven many new opportunities to disappear.

I hope... the Democrats and Republicans will work together to make the state one of the best in the nation. ... I would like to see better roads and the streets being taken care of. Better access to handicapped areas. ... I would also like for the state legislature to reduce taxes for veterans who may not be 100% disabled but those of us who are less than 100% disabled who are service connected.

We need more opportunities for those of us that barely make enough to eat but too much for any assistance. We are not asking for handouts, just a way to make it in the world... State universities should offer tuition assistance where credit is given to those who have worked full time for 5+ years in the state of Oklahoma as an incentive to go back to school. This will only help the state.

We are worried because it will affect our way of life. And where we are is the edge of two counties so a lot of the time when we have an issue we have to go back and forth between them multiple times because they keep saying it is the other county's problem.

Something like a government grant to cover living expenses as I focus on building my business would be good... It would allow me to just focus on work and not on trying to just get by day to day... I would like to see more apartments added. In small towns there are never any new apartments that are for people under 55.

Stop making people over the age of 55 get jobs; give them more food stamps to buy groceries. Also don't tell us what we can buy and not buy; we are adults... My husband has Parkinson's; I am 56 and I need to be around to take care of him.

I had my own company before and I've always worked and provided for my children... It seems like disability is extremely hard to get approved and maybe there needs to be some changes... where people that need help will look for it rather than just put it off because they think it won't happen.

You get punished for life for one mistake... it was 29 years ago and I still am being punished and still can't get a good job... My record should be dropped when my depbt to society is paid... I want a pardon from the governor... a chance at a normal life and help to get back on my feet.

We need more help for elderly and disabled to keep from being kicked out of their homes because of delinquent property tax, like me getting ready to lose my home. ... Make monthly property tax payments possible for those of us on fixed income; it's hard to try to save $2,000 to send when monthly online payments would help most of us

I would like to see better ways to obtain help. Right now it's so hard to get help, most figure, 'Why even bother?' so they do without... If it is noticed that there are people who are actually trying to help, it... shows they care and makes them feel not alone in the struggle.

Legalize recreational marijuana, tax the c--- out of it, and use those funds to eliminate state income taxes... I would love to see some revisions done to the government benefits system. I believe if the state employed a ton of counselors who would come alongside those receiving benefits, help them budget, give them guidance and encouragement... those people in the system would have enough support to dig their way out of the trenches... This helps the local economy work like a well oiled machine.

I would like for governor Stitt to not be such a selfish person. He’s not here to help those who are low income and struggle to get by... The things that help lower income people keep getting taken or made harder to get, such as the extra unemployment benefits that were being given out during Covid. The governor took those away as soon as he could along with the PEBT for children. I certainly won’t be voting for him when it comes time to vote again.

I’m a single mom working full time but I can’t qualify for food stamps. Yet, there are people qualifying for tons of foodstamps by lying their way through... In the past I’ve written the state representatives when I had an issue... and they took it serious and tried to help... The food stamps thing... just needs looked into. Penalize the liars getting a large amount and abusing the system and let the honest people be able to benefit and get help also.

I live on tribal land but do not have any rights as a tribal citizen due to my family’s lack of records... I would have access to tribal inclusion and feel as if I belonged to my tribe. It would also open doors to opportunities that my children and I have not had available to us in the past.

By enforcing these laws... the only thing that you're doing is proving to me that Oklahoma is a state that believes in complete communism... We have this wonderful thing called The Bill of Rights and, unfortunately, Oklahoma... does violate it. Forced speech and cruel and or unusual punishment are two things that happen a lot... When you're regulated to where you can live, it creates problems with being closer to your support group like your family... you really can't be a part of your family if you can't go to the park with your family... If anyone in our state's government actually had a heart, they would have repealed these a long time ago.

I remember the stress in my parents’ voices when they talked about legal paperwork, court dates, or immigration deadlines, and the sleepless nights wondering what could happen if something went wrong. ...no matter how hard we work or how much we contribute, the fear of losing safety and stability can overshadow everything else. If state leaders were listening, I would like policies that protect families from unnecessary separation and provide a clear, fair path to citizenship or legal residency. This would not only reduce fear and stress but also allow people to contribute fully to their communities, pursue education and careers, and raise their children in a safe and stable environment.

Government regulations make my career require a mandatory college degree. Many, many years ago, you could get my job from job study and working in the field to gain education and skills. But government has interfered and now that is impossible. They have also instituted boards to govern us, which is a money grab.
More help for people who need it like me. ... I've asked for assistance for years and kept being told I made $20 too much. I understand limitations but it really is hard.

For years... we had governors interested only in themselves. We now have the haves and have nots... I started working at 14 and worked most of my life... When I had to retire due to cancer, I had to move in with one of my children because I lost my house and don’t make enough to live alone... My child barely makes it and because I live with him there is help I don’t qualify for. So there is always living from check to check and no one gets ahead.

What would make it a whole lot easier for me to achieve a better tomorrow... would be if... the governor... would grant me a full pardon... I would like to see the blanket strict sex offender laws to change because the law as it is makes it almost nearly impossible for them to be able to find housing or employment... It would feel like society had finally... decided to give them the... long earned and deserved second chance that they needed to be able to become successful members of society once again.

I would like to see barriers broken down for small businesses to succeed... The city does not have places for rent for businesses that are affordable. Also there are too many regulations regarding food trucks.

I would like to see people working on cases like mine, to get my case approved for disability.... That would be all I would need to have my hopes possible. I worry daily about things I no longer have control of.

I'm disabled, and I've been fighting for 2 years now and still waiting. I've lost everything... Fix the system; it's broken... I feel like they want me to give up so they don't have to pay me. ... I worked my whole life and now the system won't help me.

To get more money on our social security checks. I am barely getting by month to month... I get $22 in food stamps a month... I eat government meals because I can’t afford healthy goods. The government meals that I have to eat are high in sodium which is not good.

Our federal government needs to stop worrying about all the other countries and focus on this one... I am currently waiting on the judge to decide my future. I am disabled but without that money I won't survive to see 50.

I'd like to see state leaders lower or eliminate taxes on everything. Lower interest rates and do away with credit checks... Then, people would be more able to get a loan for a home or vehicle without high interest amounts. They could possibly live the American dream.

Less money spent on crimes that are non-violent. Too many misdemeanor crimes lead to incarceration, therefore more prisons, staffing, and court appearances... I know so many people locked up for years over drug-related crimes. First we have to pay to take care of each person locked up. Then we have to finance the court, etc.

My age is a huge factor. The career that I’m trying for has an age cap, which I feel is unfair... I would like to... see no age caps for any profession. Everyone has different capabilities and could be an asset for any profession.
To start a non-profit organization... I would need a lawyer... to get all my permits and grants in order. I would think it would be a little harder than normal for me because of my past.
I hope we have a President and congress that help the lower income families and not the rich once. ... There are to many laws that are confusing to the older American citizens.

People who create opportunities and stability in the community should take priority and not be so easily deported... Many of my Hispanic colleagues would do better in education without the threat of family separation present.
More funding for average people that have fallen on hard times. I'm not a felon, a vet, a minority, I'm not abused, or diseased so no one will help.

I would like to see more opportunities be available to small businesses. Money that doesn't come with strings attached... Regulated businesses are a must of course but people need to feel in charge as well and be able to make decisions.

We need to let the American farmers go back to farming the way they did, before we started being governed by political people telling us what to do... We used to be able to go out and get antibiotics... for our cattle; now we are unable to do that. Now it requires that we have a prescription from a veterinarian before we can doctor our own cattle.

I would like the justice system to be audited and investigated and for them to clear house and be rebuilt so it can actually make progress instead of throwing people in prison and making money off the incarcerated people like they were cattle... For instance, the drug laws... need to be decriminalized... so they could actually receive help rather than being labeled a criminal, which will keep them a criminal usually for the rest of their lives.
I love helping others. I would love to become an RN... but no one should be forced to take any vaccines that they don’t trust.
Immigrants should not have to carry our card 24/7... They would be able to walk freely without being scared to be put in jail for 2-3 weeks because of an accent.

Progress is often delayed by slow regulatory processes... External gating from policy decisions can be the bottleneck rather than personal readiness. To accelerate progress, state leaders should break the big outcome into visible, shippable milestones and publish progress publicly to generate feedback and momentum... and use anchor commitments... to force alignment of partners and resources.
I would like medical marijuana to become recreational... There would be less cost on reapplying... and people can medicate themselves as they see fit.

Well it don’t matter what the people say, they just make the rich richer and the poor more poor... it don't matter how hard you work; if you've even been in trouble or have a record, they won’t give you the time of day. People change.

Better health care... Adults have a hard time in the health care when they don’t have health insurance because they make too much. It shouldn’t matter if you make too much; they should still qualify for health insurance.

COURTS AND COURT APPOINTED ATTORNEYS ARE HORRIBLE, THERE FOR THE $$$ NOT THE PERSON... Since Oklahoma has become a legal marijuana state, wipe all records prior to the year it became legal... DROP ALL CASES and FORCE COUNTIES TO ERASE ALL MARIJUANA CASES. It's TRAGIC TO HAVE A HISTORY WHEN ITS LEGAL NOW.
How about the government not controlling what anyone buys with Snap benefits... if Snap was available to people with a job, that would help.

There are grandchildren in college and others will be starting pre-school next year that I would like to be able to support more... Having some changes made to our local schools and daycare centers have been a huge help. There is support for school needs and lunch programs.

I would like for there to be real money invested in education... not just in my community but in the whole state.... I grew up in the 60s and 70s and we had neighborhood schools. Each neighborhood took pride in their school and many activities happened there.

I would like to see more after school activities. I see many kids in the streets and that just bothers me... More kids would be in schools and in more sports and activities. Some kids just need more encouragement from their parents and teachers.

My hope is that the education system in Oklahoma will be revamped... A lot of teachers are paying for things they need in the classroom out of their own pocket. Or they are making wish lists on Amazon to get help from anyone. Teachers should not have to take money out of their already low salary to fund their classroom... so the children and their needs become the focus of education. A teacher's budget should focus on their own family.

I think our public education has suffered more than anything in Oklahoma... I'm from a small rural town... and at the small town school that I attended as a kid, I have watched this school lose its students and state funding and end up closing and having to consolidate with nearby schools.

Being a teacher in our local church gives me a platform to help local children learn... It would help if we had tools accessible, whether online or locally to help teach and share knowledge... We need pay raises to teachers and more funding into our educational system and an opportunity to consistently have our voices heard within communities.
I'd like to see Oklahoma invest in education... I'm seeing neighbors struggle with limited school resources... and it shows better support would improve daily life.

As a teacher in Tulsa Public Schools, it is disheartening to see a lack of urgency for students who are our future... I've seen capable students fall behind due to inconsistent intervention and support... Watching students struggle not because of ability, but because of systemic gaps, makes it clear that urgent, intentional changes are needed to give them the opportunities they deserve.

We need to seriously invest in our public education as it's among the worst in the country... Their futures are being stunted as we speak. I have seen the decline in younger people's general knowledge and abilities first hand with coworkers.

I think Oklahoma needs to do better on education compared to many other states. ... We need fun and reasonably priced activities entire families can enjoy. The schools also need field trips. I feel like so much was lost during the pandemic, and many things did not return to normal regarding all of that.

I would like to see more attention paid to the education system and more support for our teachers... Our local teachers are great... Our school had more incoming students this year than previous years and our teachers deserve all the support they can get to make sure they have all the supplies and resources that they need.

Work together to increase pay for teachers and increase opportunities for kids to be kids and not simply just study to take a test that means nothing... We grew up in a time that kids were able to... have extra recesses, take field trips. We learned by hands on and experiencing not simply being able to answer a test question.

My family is made up of multiple generations of educators, so I have grown up seeing all of the shifts in education over the years. In my community, we had a misuse of funds that left our district 5 million dollars in debt, which is a disservice to students and teachers.

I hope that my kids get a great education in Oklahoma... I believe more funding needs to go to education. And the leaders need to focus on what really matters in education and stop worrying about the social issues... I'm tired of hearing bills that could be beneficial for the state - like education - not be advanced because the right side did not present it.

I would like to see the school system be genuinely interested in the learning and wellbeing of the students and to have more funding for schools... Not sending home tons of homework. The mind can only retain so much information at a time. They need a break to process information.

I’d like to see more go into schools because my nephew is in school and I’d like to know that he’s always safe and secure... I’d like to see more security but not too much because I don’t want it to feel like it’s a prison for the children, and better food and drinks for them.

I can... encourage higher education for better pay... We need better pay for our teachers and more money for our schools! We are 50th in education because money is not being handled properly and our children are suffering... We need incentives and bonuses to attract and retain teachers! School safety should also be top priority... offering professional counseling for students who are struggling... and better equipment and technology for our students
I have worked in Oklahoma in education and for DHS in Child Welfare. Our state does not provide enough resources for those who are teaching and protecting our children.
I would like to see state leaders giving back into the community, such as with food drives and even shelter for those in need and free lunches for kids.

I would like to see the state focus more on... children's education, community centers and activities that allows our children's minds to expand without being looked at or viewed by someone that's not from the same circumstances.

I have some college credits but no degree. Maybe the time to go back to school... but we rank nearly dead last in education. There hasn't been an administration in Oklahoma that has cared for public education ever since I was born.

I would like to see children's schools being properly funded... A lot of parents do not want to ship their children to the other side of town. That’s why it is important to keep the schools open in those districts.

Come sub in my classroom and see what it’s like to be a teacher. We are buying things for our classes that the school district doesn’t cover. We can barely cover our personal expenses and we’re overworked and underpaid... Our students have no problem solving skills, they can’t read, and they don’t care to learn... I teach... students in a self-contained special education classroom and they come to me not even knowing how to write a simple sentence.

I hope and dream of an Oklahoma where my future grandchildren will be educated in a free public school system that is fully funded... The rigor in public schools should be tightened without more testing. Utilize strategies from other countries that have seen the required outcome... Quit treating the education system as a child care center or a babysitter. Quit demonizing an entire industry to fit a narrative... This would change education as we know it in America … in Oklahoma. It would take a few years and we would need to commit to it with fidelity.

I would like to... pay for my children's college expenses... Spend more money on education for the public school systems... This will allow students to have the opportunity with updated educational tools like computers to grow their educational opportunities.

The downward trend in education... happened over years and was neglected for too long. Now, my grandchildren have to suffer the consequences. Maybe they will also enjoy the improvements... and get to hear news reports of how the education system is no longer the worst in the country.

We want them to fully and properly fund public education - and not the weak lip service they provide in 5 second sound bites now. ... I spent years writing, calling and visiting with legislators about important public education issues - only to be completely ignored, or given polite brushoffs or all-but-hostile dismissals.

Please get the school systems better funding and care. The kids are all of our futures... We have millions in lottery that was supposed to help schools. At least that's what the push was for the vote to approve it.
I hope to help my children advance to high school and prepare for college in Oklahoma... Reading is fundamental... my children will thrive in education... if we have the correct resources.

I want to see this state have better schools, that means MORE money for schools. … Student loan debt. Cancel it all! Kids get free college now. Oklahoma’s promise. Why am I still in debt at 52 for something my son and grandson got for free??? I still don’t own a home because my d---a-- went to college! … We’re dead last in education and the very top in incarceration of women. You can’t figure that correlation out, can you?

Better education.. there's so many young people being arrested for horrible crimes. Why aren't they in school? Where's the funding to get these people off the streets and educated? ... Since Covid kids are just not in school... Where is funding for YMCA's or stuff like that for after-school programs? Where's all our money going? We're not even fixing our roads or our kids; it's all going to military and prisons.
...making education more accessible for the community since we live in a high poverty area.
Oklahoma has a horrible education system. I would hope to see the money they get from taxes go to the children's education... To be honest, it would probably be the lunch room.
I would like to see our tax money being allocated towards things that actually matter, like... education... We have been consistently low ranking amongst the 50 states in education.

It would be nice if they could figure out a way to make school lunches free for everyone... The jails and prisons can get 3 meals a day then why can’t the kids? ... Sadly for some, that’s the only meal they might receive.
I would like to see an increase in our school budgets to give our children a better and higher education... for better future job opportunities for kids with a better education.
I wish I could help my grandchildren get more education... Some have been cheated out of their educations.
Increase education funding.... Education is important and we were driven into the ground by Walters.
I would like to see more schools for our children... We need more schools instead of closing them down.

Education... Trying to make sure every class room has a Trump bible in it doesn't help anyone... that money could be used for other things like free meals for needy families or better programs in school.

Bring back real education and teachers... We need actual updated books and materials... paying our teachers livable wages so they can be motivated to teach, and programs for at risk children to get the help and resources they need.

State legislation is focused more on building a bigger city... rather than focusing on building stronger foundations in education. This... leads to a weaker infrastructure and a weaker economy due to individuals with an education leaving to pursue better things outside the state.
Prioritize more funding for education and get us out of the hole we're in... Hire high quality educators and attract them to the state or keep them here with competitive salaries.

There needs to be... a step forward in technology in education, and we need to teach forward thinking to the youth... With the introduction of the new solar farm, it forces the hand that we’re gonna need people to maintain it. We need youth to come in... and the only way that it’s gonna move forward is by educating and re-educating... so we need more vocational schools with relevant vocational skills being taught.
We should invest in all schools here to upgrade ... because schools are where our kids are half a day; they need to be safe and protected.
I would like to see more funding being provided for our education system... so that schools could do more for the students and we could have better teachers and improve the curriculum.
I hope for... education... for all of my family.
I think all school lunches should be free.

I hope they decide to help teachers with a pay raise, and better funding for schools.... With better funding for schools, their children get a better education. This will allow their children a better future.
You should start funding the schools more... and start understanding special needs better... It would... help special needs people to get jobs and do stuff regular people can.
I would like to see... all families having the same access to education no matter where in the state they live.
I hope for... better schools...
I hope that they all have a good education and are happy.
Better... education... is an area that needs to improve for the benefit of us all.
Better education.
This state does not care about... the education of our youth...

It's a tragedy that Oklahoma as a whole is 50th in education... I went to school here in Oklahoma, both high school and vocational school... Despite our general education being lowly rated, at Vo-tech they bragged that Oklahoma is one of the best states... and it showed. There's no reason why public schools can't be that way as well if they're funded and treated right.

I think people need more financial education... I am getting close to retirement age and I wish I had learned how to take care of my finances earlier. I am 57 years old and I don't have near the amount of money... that will make me be able to afford retiring at a decent age. These aren't abstract policy issues—they're life-and-death realities... What has been especially moving is seeing how tribal nations in Oklahoma step up during these crises to provide food assistance... to anyone in need—not just tribal members, but the entire surrounding community. When the federal government fails, tribal food distribution programs... become lifelines for people of all backgrounds... The lesson is that Oklahoma's state government should adopt the same commitment to community wellbeing that tribal nations demonstrate... Oklahoma can be a place where no senior citizen skips meals to afford medicine... and where government shutdowns don't threaten anyone's ability to eat.
