Seniors can be a very very important part of a community, providing wisdom and ideas that pave the way to reduce burdens for people that enter the senior cohort after them.

I've watched neighbors ration insulin, seen families choose between groceries and prescriptions, witnessed the fear in seniors’ eyes during government shutdowns when their SNAP benefits were threatened, and heard stories of people traveling out of state for healthcare they should be able to access here. These aren't abstract policy issues—they're life-and-death realities... What has been especially moving is seeing how tribal nations in Oklahoma step up during these crises to provide food assistance... to anyone in need—not just tribal members, but the entire surrounding community. When the federal government fails, tribal food distribution programs... become lifelines for people of all backgrounds... The lesson is that Oklahoma's state government should adopt the same commitment to community wellbeing that tribal nations demonstrate... Oklahoma can be a place where no senior citizen skips meals to afford medicine... and where government shutdowns don't threaten anyone's ability to eat. That's the Oklahoma I want to live in, and that's the Oklahoma our leaders have the power to create if they choose people over politics.

I would like to see an increase in funding for DDS services... and incentives for... employers that more freely hire individuals with disabilities. I work with individuals with a disability and finding employment for them is very difficult because of the stigma.... Employing individuals with disabilities, actually decreases employee turnaround and creates a better work environment for other coworkers.

Damages from a home in disrepair can cause sickness, and mold to grow, which effects a person's health and well being. I have Lupus and day by day things can be a struggle. ... When those repairs happen, their health and mental well being increase and in the long run save the state money on health care for low income and disabled families.

I am 62. I would love to retire and actually enjoy the rest of my life... without having to worry where the next meal comes from... I live in a community with mostly older citizens. It hurts to see how we all struggle to put food on the table and eat healthy diets if we can not afford it. Also, I think Social Security should be more than it is right now; we paid in all our lifes.

Right now I am disabled so definitely we need healthcare reform, better in network healthcare and choices. It's hard to live on a small budget on disability... I think getting out of our homes to do things with others... would help our minds which is also good for our physical health.

I have experience with the homeless population and I have found that a lot of them desire a different kind of lifestyle but are unsure where to start to better themselves... I would like to see more assistance and types of housing to help our veterans and homeless population... and Government programs that offer basic on the job skills training... A law forbidding misdemeanor background checks would be a great start.

I would hope that my grandson would be tested and have a real diagnosis and treatment. The schools kept losing him when help was asked for and he still needs it... My grandson would like to get a job and earn a living but as it stands now, no one would want to hire a socially awkward and anxious, possibly on the autism spectrum, young man... Get the children tested that need it. Don’t let them fall in the cracks.

My hope is that... I will be able to walk better... I would like to see better... access to handicapped areas. It gives those of us who are handicapped access to recreational areas. I would also like for the state legislature to reduce taxes for veterans who... are less than 100% disabled who are service connected.

I have many medical issues that keep me from having a job and I have been denied disability three times... proper medication is hard to come by due to my insurance which is SoonerSelect, and it doesnâ€™t allow me to see a sleep specialist because it doesnâ€™t cover that type of specialist... Iâ€™m not properly medicated for my narcolepsy and I cannot drive more than 30 minutes by myself... I just feel that younger people get judged on filing for disability... I donâ€™t feel that the Social Security Disability Administration looks at anybody that is younger with PTSD and narcolepsy and severe anxiety and depression as someone who should be considered disabled... I feel like they are gonna contribute to the narcotic and opioid epidemic by saying, 'oh well you can go to the pain management and deal with what ails you and continue working.' My mental health is definitely taking a toll and I would love nothing more than to get my children a place to call home.

A lot of senior citizens have to choose between eating and buying life saving drugs, this should not even be an issue in this country... I was without insurance when I had a stroke... I'm still getting bills in the thousands... The medication that I needed had to be purchased by someone else because I couldn't afford it.

My health has in the past few years went down extremely... It seems like disability is extremely hard to get approved... there is a stigma in Oklahoma that when you file a disability you're automatically going to get disapproved... People that need help will look for it rather than just put it off because they think it won't happen.

I would like to see the availability of resources for family, children, and elderly and disabled people. I just believe that they are overlooked and underserved... I'm disabled now. However, I was born abled bodied so I've seen both sides, and I'm very dissatisfied with the availability of resources for the handicapped and disabled.

What makes it harder is a lot of politicians think Social Security is a handout. It is not a handout; it is our money... It is very irritating that some politicians see it as a handout, especially since politicians don't pay into social security but have their own retirement system set up. I think it is very unfair and politicians should participate in the same program that all other Americans have to.

I would like to see my wife healed of a chronic pain and eye problems... Dropping property and state income taxes for seniors would make more funds available for health, medical and other needs instead of paying taxes.

As a disabled vet, it is harder to do more of the manual labor jobs I'd like to do because of the pain they cause, and at the same time, it currently feels like we can have our benefits targeted and stripped over ridiculous politically charged conversations.

A lot of us senior citizens have to make choices between actually buying groceries or do without because of the high cost of medical help or medications... It would be a godsend. The seniors could breathe a little better and improve their daily life. Being able to buy the little extras like toilet paper or a garbage can is very satisfying

If my husband and I did not have to pay our Medicare premium... plus our prescription plan and supplemental plan... we pay over 700.00 a month! We could save more to have more financially security. We wouldn't have to worry when we have to spend money on a necessity or when we have to replace something around the house.

If there were some sort of support for people that were diagnosed as adults with autism. They provide a lot of support to kids diagnosed now, and I really could have used that to help me find a viable career when I was young and able to go to school.

We need more help for elderly and disabled to keep from being kicked out of their homes because of delinquent property tax (like me getting ready to lose my home)... Make monthly property tax payments possible for those of us on fixed income, it's hard to try to save $2,000 to send when monthly online payments would help most of us

I would like to see us keeping up with the elderly neighbors I have to make sure they have enough to eat... Being a good neighbor is something I take personally because I would want someone to help me when I get old and have trouble doing it myself.

I hope better treatment for cancer is found... We need to bring more awareness to programs for our veterans and help people to get off the streets and start towards a better life... We see it in every day life, people just needing that one break, that one person that will give them a chance.

I live in a rural area and access to good healthcare is difficult. As a senior, public transportation from my rural home to Tulsa would help me access the medical help I need... It would improve my health by ensuring I can make my doctor appointments and relieve the severe stress I encounter when driving on Tulsa’s busy streets.

I worked 30 years at a job and just can't do it anymore. I applied for disability but how do you survive during the process? ... Instead of being sent for a physical to a stranger who doesn't even take the time to actually...complete a physical on you...they get the info wrong on the paperwork. ... You get denied. Then you have to wait on appeal. How are we expected to survive in the meantime?

As the state ages, its citizenry is also aging. I would like to feel much more secure and benefits for senior citizens. ... As a senior citizen in a senior living community, I find that many people are unhappy with the status quo...
I have severe arthritis in my back and spine... Walking helps my back and mind. Mentally it helps me a lot. I take medication to help me do the things I like to do.

I would like it for people that are low income to have a better chance on being able to own a home... because some of us are on a fixed income... and it's hard for us to put money aside when we can't rely on any other income during the month besides what we get on the first.

Attention to the homelessness question and attention to the housing situation. The present cost of housing is extremely high. We need to be able for young newly married people to buy a small home instead of living with their parents... Raising the rent yearly should not happen. Seniors are priced out of their apartments when they live a long time... they and most people need to be left in their apartment with the rent stable so they don't need to move. Having to move is expensive and more difficult than most can manage.

After over 50 years on the work force, I find my monthly social security payment is too low. ... It is hard to make all your payments on time, when the money comes in in the middle of the month. ... Life after working over 50 years, should not be so hard.
I had to get a job at age 56 away from my husband who has Parkinson's... I would be able to... stay home with my husband... if I had more help with food and his disability check was a little higher.

I would like to see people with disabilities not have to wait so long for approval to get disability checks... I would not make the paperwork so time consuming and difficult. Plus, I would like for caregivers in Oklahoma to get paid to stay home and care for a family member with a disability.

I'd like a healthy sense of being... and to see more resources for the homeless and veterans. I believe they're not heard and overlooked... You can't help but see it everywhere. Just someone to care enough.

They are stripping our benefits right and left. Our mental health care, our... medical benefits, food, our housing…where has it all gone? ... We can’t afford food at my house most weeks. We have beans and noodles to get by. ... I want to see this state have... better health care, especially mental health services. FUND that! People who are retired and over the age of 64 should not have to beg pain doctors for a minutia of the medication they need

I currently am not able to work because I need a new hip... I worked as a caregiver and would like to return to that... Even though I'm 82, I don't want to spend my last years figuring out what I'll watch on TV. I want to be useful.

They told us it would be at least 3 years for a new home because we are disabled... I hoped to be drawing my disability by then. With those benefits, we would not be struggling as much... We would be able to breathe a sigh of relief and take care of ourselves better.

The USA's social security infrastructure is crumbled... It's harder to get your benefits, that is if you get them at all before you die... I have yet to see this change. People are dying while being rejected for... social security not one, not two, maybe three times or more and being put on hold for hours having to get lawyers. It's ridiculous.

I have been diagnosed with TBI and Post Concussion Syndrome... and have been unable to apply for disability due to the massive amount of mental effort required in doing so. My hope is to find a job that is able to support my medical needs and my responsibilities financially.

I have been dealing with anxiety/panic disorder for 12 years now. I hope that in the future I can get it under control enough so I can get a job... and provide for my family. ... I rarely leave my house and I'm not currently working. ... I am autistic and I am sensitive to noises more than the average person.

I would like to see the homelessness crisis addressed as well as awareness brought to those with physical disabilities or unable to work due to circumstances. These problems are not discussed enough by legislators to an efficient degree to get anything done in my opinion.

I am disabled which will make it hard to find work. More companies need job opportunities for disabled people... I know too many people, myself included, that are one paycheck away from being homeless.

My Social Security disability payments stopped... without warning. I have terminal cancer and it was my only firm of income. I became homeless... With what happened I lost everything including hope... The programs available are stretched beyond need... I had DHS joke with me about being homeless... I got jokes instead of serious helpful suggestions.

I am 65 years old and currently live with and take care of my 86 year old Father. In 5-10 years I hope to still be able to have health care I can afford... I am on a fixed income, so... a supplemental insurance to go with Medicare that is affordable would also help.

I would like to see more help for seniors... I would not have to make the choice of paying my bills or getting my sick animal taken care of. It would be a blessing to not have to worry if my pets are going to have to suffer.

For people who are on SSI that have children to get better help... Getting my truck fixed is my most important thing... I would feel like an adult and feel like a good mom and a responsible person. And I think my daughter would feel better about me. She would be able to rely on me again.

I am in a very rural area... I can’t drive and can’t have a strict schedule as I have focal epilepsy that has disabled me. The towns have buses for things like that but not where I am and it’s isolating. There are a lot of elderly and disabled that suffer because of this.

For months I’ve been trying to get my wheelchair repaired... I am signed up to receive shelf stable food. That only came once... I understand there is a big need for services, but I’m also asking for help. If I knew I was being listened to or what kind of arrangements are being made, I could be more patient.
I would like to see our government help our vets... I have seen vets not able to get the care that they have earned by fighting for our country because our government doesn't care.

We need to ease up programs that help the needy. They are the most vulnerable... Many think getting work is simple but that is not always the case... I had an easier time getting work when I was younger, but it seems the older you get the harder it becomes.
I'm on disability. I cannot work... Stop blowing our food stamps and our healthcare benefits... little things like that make a lot of difference for people that live from day to day.

Being able to get my birth certificate, social security replacement card and state ID is currently out of my control... If I had these three items I would be able to get disability started... so I could feel more safe and secure without a fight or flight constant feeling.

I think taking care of the most vulnerable people in our society is very important... Because there are people with endless potential that are overlooked because of poverty. People that could offer a lot to our communities but feel like they can't be heard, so they don't speak... You should not feel inadequate because you're experiencing a financial crisis or because you are old.

I started working at 14 and worked most of my life and raised 4 children by myself. When I had to retire due to cancer, I had to move in with one of my children because I lost my house and don’t make enough to live alone... My child barely makes it and because I live with him there is help I don’t qualify for. So there is always living from check to check and no one gets ahead.

I’ve been a hard worker all my life until like 5 years ago. Now I’ve had strokes and have neuropathy, fibromyalgia, diabetes, and a lot of other issues that prevent me from working... I could buy shampoo, hair color, soap that actually smells good. It’s the little things that you miss, that make you a completely different person... I want to thrive in this disabled state. Just surviving sucks

I hope for more resources for children with Autism. We’re already secluded enough as it is without these services... If I could send my son to school. He is nonverbal autistic and it’s hard finding a good school around here. But none of that is in my control.

I applied for disability 5 months ago, and have heard nothing... If I had the necessities and medical expenses covered, I wouldn't be stressed out as much on a daily basis... That would be all I would need to have my hopes possible.
I have a father that’s disabled and I feel like the city needs more sidewalks... It would make it easier for people with disabilities to travel.

I want to be helping out my community’s... elderly... but we need better job opportunities for the disabled like me. I have autism and... I've been in school a lot so I don't have a lot of experience.

I sit here waiting to be housed with major health issues and need someone to help me file for social security... and I can't even get a case worker. ... No one is going to hear me... Just when you think something might go your way, they run out of funds.

I would like to see that when the weather is bad some one to take care of the older people... That they have heat or blankets to keep them warm doing the cold winter months. and cooling systems that work doing the hot months.

I want to be able to finally get my SSI so I can make it day to day and live the right way and not by relying on others... I wish my back pain would ease up so I can complete day to day tasks and not rely on others to help or help me manage things in my life.

I would like to see us do something to help the homeless rather than running them off... I see homeless veterans all the time when going to the city... They protected our freedoms and now we aren’t taking care of them.

I would like to see more funds and programs to help the homeless veterans and everyone that lives on the street get on their feet so that they have a chance to take care of themselves... I believe they deserve the respect of helping them when they helped us.

So many people I know don’t have health insurance; they can’t afford it and they fall through the cracks... There are a lot of elderly who are still working at age 80 just to make ends meet. They should not be working; they should be at home gardening and cooking and relaxing.
We need more help for the elderly and those on fixed incomes... Everyday struggles are real for many who have to decide whether to be warm, eat, or get meds.

Nursing homes... need to be better regulated. $4,000 a month for my aunt is appalling for her to pay. And when her money runs out, the state has to take over... The greed is too rampant... Regulators need to have watchdogs... to be made sure the regulations are kept.

Really not much I can do myself because of my health. ... I'm not looking to get rich, I just want to be able to live the rest of my life comfortably. Right now I'm in debt and can't afford to keep standard things going, such as keeping warm.
Working from home will allow me to care for my ill spouse who cannot work while making needed extra income for our household.
I have had a difficult time ever since I was discharged from the Army in 2014... Get control of my gambling. I spend too much gambling right now.

Living on a set income and being disabled is not fun. ... When I ask for help, they say, 'You make to much money and we can’t help you'. I've had that happen a lot. I'm unable to work because of the pain I’m in all the time.

We need to help people that suffer from multiple seizures to be able to get disability without... being turned down every time that they apply... If they felt supported by these systems, they would feel more worthwhile and not feel so closed off from everything and everyone around them; they would be able to have a life.
I hope for a future where seniors are stabilized... Seniors get hit triple by inflation, increased taxes, and increased instability for Social Security which makes it more difficult.
I have too much physical problems to do the things I've always done in jobs I've had... The homeless and drug problems are pretty bad in town... Of course the homeless need more help.
My chronic pain is outside of my control, and at times it scares me... I have achieved a lot in my youth, but now I physically can no longer finish any achievements.
I'm disabled, I've been fighting for 2 years now and still waiting. I've lost everything... I worked my whole life and now the system won't help me.

I think there needs to be more mental health support throughout the state. I also believe that there should be more resources for people who are struggling financially due to inability to work from disability/not being able to find a job... people would be able to have overall more stable lives than they do now, thus stabilizing the people around them better as well.

I’m currently in recovery after being in a car accident so I'm just mainly focused on getting better and learning how to walk again... I'm super independent and having to ask for help is hard for me to do.
I want to see... our veterans who need more help... We still have veterans... who are homeless and not cared for as promised by the state and federal government.
I'm tired of hearing about victories in elections and who controls what house. Let's move forward and celebrate achievements in healthcare and senior entitlements.
I’ve known elderly who only get $24 in food stamps and no family to help them. That’s not enough to live off of.

We have to do something about the homeless instead of just moving them around or putting them in jail... Most of them are Vietnam Vets so let's build homes and help them with mental health issues. We need to train our police force on how to deal with mental health.
In 5-10 years I want my husband & I to be healthy & happy... The threat of losing Social Security & Medicare is a huge threat to my family.

A lot of people who can no longer work wouldn't end up losing most every thing that they worked their lives for all because they were denied what they paid in on SSI and disability until it was too late and they already lost most everything.

One thing that makes it harder to achieve a better tomorrow right now is my physical health. ... An improved physical well being would help me be able to work longer hours. By doing this I would be able to save more and be able to achieve those goals.

I hope for a better healthcare system and more disability funding and programs for disabled families... Stop screwing with disabled people’s income... I'm sick and tired of living on nothing then the government takes even more away.

Getting better health care insurance, dental care coverage, and vision care coverage... would relieve a lot of anxiety about current and future health issues... The inadequacy of my retirement after 25 years of teaching.

Our veterans are not treated the way they should be. ... There shouldnâ€™t be any homeless Veterans. They should have housing. ... They have sacrificed their lives for us. Iâ€™m a veteran... and I just think we need to take care of them.
Due to my health I can not work much... I just need someone that understands what I am going through, and will be there for comfort. The finances will figure out.
I am currently waiting on the judge to decide my future... I am disabled but without that money I won't survive to see 50.

Seniors could stay in their home and not be worried how to afford grocery, medications or upkeep... We need more programs on electrificity and natural gas, farmer market and grocery benefits with improved benefits on Medicare programs.
I hope to be staying smoke free and getting a home to live in... and working pain free daily.

We are at a stand still in getting a new home due to myself only being on disability... I don’t want to pass away in this crappy, holey mobile home... We just don’t have the right funds or cosigner (for me being on disability)
The homelessness... it's sad, it's really out of control... Turn abandoned buildings into shelters... no one cares... No veteran should... have to die being cold.
I want to be able to see my grandkids finish school, get married and have families of their own... We also need more support for Older Americans.

I’m worrying about social security benefits and if they will be around when I get that age. ... In my life I haven’t kept a job long enough to build a retirement, so social security is very important to me so I can live.
If I was physically in better shape and not having pain, I would be able to work... but I'm not.

I would like to see folks on disability be able to afford housing, food, doctor visits and prescriptions without having to decide which to pay for and which to do without. ... If everyone could have the basics of their needs met, I believe that crime rates would drop and drug uses would also subside.

I hope that Social Security will still be supported by the government... Lawmakers need to secure our future by voting to protect Social Security now and in the future... and protect senior citizens by making sure that Social Security is protected.
I would love to still be here in my right mind with my husband and not a burden to anyone for the rest of my life.

Make sure laws are in place to help seniors and others as they age so they will not have to struggle and worry about the future... Not having good health care and not being able to afford food and shelter are struggles for lots of people.
Figuring out the medical insurances and helping the poor and elderly would be beneficial... it would allow the poor and elderly a chance to eat AND buy their prescriptions.

More help for the homeless and hungry people of this community... As a country, we send more aid to countries that need medical, housing and food... and neglect our own... If you're gonna begin helping the needy, the homeless and the elderly, you have to start somewhere and why not close to where you live?
The state can start understanding special needs better... It would... help special needs people to get jobs and do stuff regular people can.
I want to see our local and state leaders... to feed the poor and protect the weak. ... No underage sex changes. Taking care of our seniors and our veterans.
Leaders should be doing what they said they would do when we voted for them: to help the homeless and help with healthcare and social security disability.

I am no longer in a domestic violence situation and was homeless for 2 years upon leaving the marriage. I learned first hand the challenges faced and what was available to my needs. For example, housing, shelters for abuse victims, food, counseling, transportation, medical and dental care without insurance or money... My former abuse issues may no longer exist but I have never fully recovered financially and cannot work full time because I am now on Social Security and am physically limited... By giving my time and energy volunteering, it gives me a sense of peace and happiness... Hopefully I live long enough to see... more resources for those who truly need it.

As a person on a fixed income due to being on disability, it is nearly impossible to increase my income... I recently had to replace the tires on my vehicle as they were literally worn to the point they would not hold air. I had to borrow money from a relative to be able to get new ones, and then had to sell personal items to pay them back... The state needs to increase access to food funds like SNAP and housing and rent subsidies for low-income residents.

We need places to go for homeless people... it's hard to see these people struggle with nowhere to go. ... I have worked at Love's and Chisholm Corner and it breaks my heart seeing them go hungry and thirsty, so I buy them food and give them drinks because I know how bad it feels to go hungry.

The harder thing is my diagnosed social phobia or anxiety disorder. I have an extra hurdle that many other students don't have... The main issue I would like to see... is the homeless population. We need to expand our services and provide services for many people who are struggling with mental illness... The ones I see near my apartment are clearly in distress because of delusions. They shout at the sky... and they need help.

I know what it's like to suffer and nobody will help... I would like to see more support for the homeless and for youth. It is very cold on the streets right now in Oklahoma and the people who have found themselves without a place to call home will die if nobody steps up and helps them. I would like to see more compassion here for people less fortunate.

I would like a better program for the homeless people. ... It's really hard to see some of them out in the cold in really bad winter because of max capacity in the shelters. A rehab program or even a job placement program would be very helpful for them. I feel like it's harder to get jobs once they have been homeless for a long time.

I would like to see more programs that help the homeless become more successful and off the streets so that their family are warm during the winter.... My family started out homeless when they first moved here. They were able to make friends who guided them into the right places for getting the help they needed.

I have experience with the homeless population and I have found that a lot of them desire a different kind of lifestyle but are unsure where to start to better themselves... I would like to see more assistance and types of housing to help our veterans and homeless population... and Government programs that offer basic on the job skills training... A law forbidding misdemeanor background checks would be a great start.

I feel like our small town has a lot of homeless people and our town really needs a homeless shelter. ... The food bank can be helpful but we have one that gives out old yucky food... Well, they wouldn't be digging in trash cans; maybe they can have a shower, a warm bed, also find a job and get on their feet.

Coming from someone who has had to use our food banks, the food quality is awful. A lot of the food is either almost expired, if not already. Also, to add fuel to the fire, a lot of the food that people take gets thrown away because they don't have a proper way to store it... We should ask about their situation, what they need, what would help them, not just get a name for the system and send them on their merry way. It goes so much deeper. Why do you think that person is homeless or hungry to begin with? They need help.

I would like for our community... to help each other out when others are suffering... I've learned the importance of this from seeing homeless people on the street and offering to get them some food or just a lil cheap outfit.

In 5-10 years I'm hoping that I would have my own place and be able to maintain it without help... One thing I would like to see happen in our state is the housing or HUD to open back up bc I'm facing homelessness... When I was chosen to get a voucher the first time it was a shock to me about how I have to figure out a way to come up with my deposits and application fees... and when my voucher was supposed to expire I was needing more time... but housing never gave me an extension.

I'd like to see DHS brought under good leadership, with fewer children taken out of their homes unnecessarily... and I'd like to see it NOT BE ILLEGAL TO BE "HOMELESS." ... I have struggled to keep a roof my entire life and lived without one many times. It's difficult enough to survive, without laws like that.

We need more help for the homeless people throughout the state. It seems like everywhere I go I see someone sitting up against a building with all their belongings and no place to go... Rent has increased to a point to where some people working can't afford to put a roof over their head. This is a real problem in small and large towns.

I’d like to see more mental health and community services that are easy to access... to give people a chance to get back on their feet instead of just surviving day to day. My personal experience was I've been homeless before... My parents and my brother are currently homeless... As far as mental health, I personally have mental health problems and my mom has so many... she is a schizophrenic and schizoaffective... and does not know what reality is... With her having these problems along with being homeless, people avoid her or try to attack her, and my parents and brother don't deserve to be laying in the middle of winter... in a tent like a dog not knowing when they will be able to shower or even eat.

I always provided health insurance for my family and I was able to keep them on my plan longer with the ACA and would think most parents find this a comfort... I would like to see healthcare, housing and food security for families with children... When people have food and housing they feel less desperate and safer which leads to less depression, anxiety and anger.

I hope they address the homeless... There has to be something we can do to help the less fortunate. My grand daughter sees them and wants to help, so I think other children and people in our community want to help them as well.

I am hopeful that all of the homeless people in our state will be able to have safe places to live and the funds available in order to thrive instead of barely surviving... I'm going to start an online company... to help accomplish this, but the challenge is the inability to obtain the necessary funding... I'd like to see... not only state and local government but also our federal government joining in this movement... because I know from past experiences that it's one thing to have local or even state support; however, it carries more weight if the federal housing is backing these proposals as well.

Right behind my house is a train track and it seems like ever since the Covid virus happened, the homeless people problem has gotten worse. There is a small village of a homeless camp behind my house... what they need is to have more homeless shelters so they don't need to live next to the railroad tracks. If they were all to move into a shelter, they would be safe and we would definitely be much safer without them living there.

We need more help for mental illness. I experienced this in my family with... no beds in mental health facilities because there are so few places to go... It would definitely help with the homeless population because 75 percent of the homeless just need help with their mental illness.

I would like to see more resources for the poor. More housing choices, yes, but we deserve clean and safe housing just like everyone else... I'm currently on housing and every place available is always dirty, full of bugs, dilapidated, or in a very unsafe neighborhood.

I need to make sure that my mental and well-being is prioritized... We need to focus more on homeless needs, children's education, community centers and activities that allows our children's minds to expand without being looked at or judged by someone that's not from the same circumstances.

My honest opinion is that the system has many flaws... I've seen first hand... the effect of improper care for mentally ill individuals. They are admitted into a care facility, treated for a short time then released out into the streets to fend for themselves with no food, shelter, or money.
We need to guide the homeless in the right direction, to the right resource... I myself have been homeless and took advantage of the resources. They are there if you want them.

What I truly craved was a compassionate space where I could receive the help that would enable me to heal and rebuild my life.
My social anxiety makes things a lot harder... Having my sisters around me helps. I feel less anxious and know if something bad happens they’ll help.
I'm 25, had to lose a son to adoption and am addicted to drugs... I need to start my life.

I see too many broken families where the children are forced into fostercare or turn to drugs and drinking. If services are provided to help these families get the help they need, it would be a start to helping keep the drug problem down and less juvenile offenders.

I think there needs to be more mental health support throughout the state. I also believe that there should be more resources for people who are struggling financially due to inability to work from disability/not being able to find a job... people would be able to have overall more stable lives than they do now, thus stabilizing the people around them better as well.

We have to do something about the homeless instead of just moving them around or putting them in jail... Most of them are Vietnam Vets so let's build homes and help them with mental health issues. We need to train our police force on how to deal with mental health.

Things that make it harder to achieve my goals would be... getting stuck in despair of feeling like it will never happen... I think that one thing I would like to see happen in my city would be less homeless people... because some have mental illnesses and or are on drugs.

What's out of my control is my health in this moment. I’m focused on healing... I would also like to see mental health focusing because this society has taught people that vulnerability is weakness and instead of dealing with our own psyche, we take it out on others.
My hope is to quit drugs and to get a job and to do that I would have to be sober... I'm not sure how. I'm trying to figure that out day by day... I have bad luck so.

In... 5/10 years one thing I hope my family has is peace with each other and everyone being happy and sober. ... I would hope I got my stuff together in order to make a home for me and my unborn child... I can’t provide because of personal stuff going on but I would love to have a house and peace finally.

I think that it’s an absolute travesty that the state would... cut the funding for mental health. Mental health should not be so stigmatized and treated like it’s something to be ashamed of or embarrassed about, because it’s real and for so many people, it can have almost debilitating effects in day to day life.
I think it's hard for me to know what I should be doing. I'm not particularly smart or good at anything really... Everything I try I'm bad at and hate. It's really difficult... I've lost hope.

I wish I didn’t have a penis, I wish I was a girl, but hey, can't do nothing about it now can I? ... This is because of gender dysphoria, depression and being broke... I would like to see anti trans laws get abolished, and to bring back food stamps... because hatefulness is wrong.
I hope to be staying smoke free and getting a home to live in... and working pain free daily.
I hope that in five years I will have all five of my babies home with me... I need to be getting completely clean... and have a stable home, transportation, and income.
A better tomorrow for me would be clean and sober... I would feel so much better about not just life but myself.

In our state, education, mental health, addiction, and homelessness all need to be looked at... If you really look at it... all of these issues are related. If you don't receive a good education... you aren't going to be furthering your education, which means an extremely low wage job or no job. Which brings in the addiction and homelessness. And there are mental health issues everywhere.
I see homeless people literally crawling the streets, laying on benches, on the ground. Beggars on every corner, people having outbursts in the streets and just being arrested and not helped.

It shouldn’t be an instant jail sentence if I... have a couple of puffs to ease my nerves while I was driving... The back of my med card says I can have up to 3oz on my person so who the h--- do you think you are telling me what I can and can’t do? Weed isn’t evil; the Baptists that run the Oklahoma government need to... realize that... reefer madness is silly government propaganda.
It would be easier if I was not having anxiety around people... It would also be easier if felons could get decent jobs.

He has never been in trouble until he was 35 and it was from a drug addiction... He shouldn’t be looked at as a felon the rest of his life because of an addiction he had never gotten treatment for. Addiction is terrible; people deserve a second chance... We should make it to where Oklahoma is known as a second chance state.

I hope for the ability to break the cycle of emotional and mental abuse... to freely talk about feelings and mental issues and stop the nonsense of the physical abuse and higher expectations if you’re a female.
It would help people a vastly amount because a lot of people are struggling and killing themselves stressing to figure out how to get what they need.
I am currently in the situation and before this I would have never thought this could be me. ... Then it’s like, okay, I’m homeless now; how do I get off drugs if I’m homeless?

If I could fast forward... we would have universal healthcare... and it would be affordable... You could also have paid mental health subsidies. If we are mentally prepared, we would be better off for the future.
I hope we have multiple kids who are thriving, learning and loving life.... We need to prioritize... mental health of children and parents...

I think expanding mental health program funding would make the state and Norman safer... There would likely be less homelessness, and less incidents of crime if there were adequate mental health infrastructure in place.

The drug laws... need to be decriminalized... so that it’s not bad looking for someone to be an addict so they could actually receive help rather than being labeled a criminal which will keep them a criminal usually for the rest of their lives. ... It would be a miracle for so many people to finally be able to be treated like people again rather than pushed away and forgotten about or persecuted for the rest of their lives.
Stop taking people to jail for petty drug crimes... It would better the whole community if the drug addicts got help instead of sent to prison where there's only more drugs.
We need to help the homeless... and stop over packing prisons. Mental health is needed. If the person isn't violent, why cage them like an animal?

I am no longer in a domestic violence situation and was homeless for 2 years upon leaving the marriage. I learned first hand the challenges faced and what was available to my needs. For example, housing, shelters for abuse victims, food, counseling, transportation, medical and dental care without insurance or money... My former abuse issues may no longer exist but I have never fully recovered financially and cannot work full time because I am now on Social Security and am physically limited... By giving my time and energy volunteering, it gives me a sense of peace and happiness... Hopefully I live long enough to see... more resources for those who truly need it.

I have no retirement so I depend on social security and Medicare. My copayments for medicine and doctor visits take about a third of my monthly income. I make just over the cutoff line for State aid... Grocery prices themselves have risen so dramatically that I cannot afford even the cheapest meats. Thank goodness for food banks... I would like to see social security benefits be risen for seniors like me so that we can buy food and medical without having to give up something to pay for them.

I've lost so many family members at young ages... I'm all about eating healthy and living a long life for my grandbabies, but this world seems to make junk food eating cheap and if you want to eat healthy it costs a lot more money... I would love to see healthy food cost less... My best memories are growing my own food with my family. I'd do it again today if more people would eat the way we used to.

As a person on a fixed income due to being on disability, it is nearly impossible to increase my income... I recently had to replace the tires on my vehicle as they were literally worn to the point they would not hold air. I had to borrow money from a relative to be able to get new ones, and then had to sell personal items to pay them back... The state needs to increase access to food funds like SNAP and housing and rent subsidies for low-income residents.

We need better options for those on Medicare... Not all counties offer these things. I live in Washington County and our options are VERY limited... I believe they could thrive, having the ability to purchase healthy food and options for over the counter pharmacy needs which would impact better health.

I've watched neighbors ration insulin, seen families choose between groceries and prescriptions, witnessed the fear in seniors’ eyes during government shutdowns when their SNAP benefits were threatened, and heard stories of people traveling out of state for healthcare they should be able to access here. These aren't abstract policy issues—they're life-and-death realities... What has been especially moving is seeing how tribal nations in Oklahoma step up during these crises to provide food assistance... to anyone in need—not just tribal members, but the entire surrounding community. When the federal government fails, tribal food distribution programs... become lifelines for people of all backgrounds... The lesson is that Oklahoma's state government should adopt the same commitment to community wellbeing that tribal nations demonstrate... Oklahoma can be a place where no senior citizen skips meals to afford medicine... and where government shutdowns don't threaten anyone's ability to eat. That's the Oklahoma I want to live in, and that's the Oklahoma our leaders have the power to create if they choose people over politics.

I am 62. I would love to retire and actually enjoy the rest of my life... without having to worry where the next meal comes from... I live in a community with mostly older citizens. It hurts to see how we all struggle to put food on the table and eat healthy diets if we can not afford it. Also, I think Social Security should be more than it is right now; we paid in all our lifes.

I feel like our small town has a lot of homeless people and our town really needs a homeless shelter. ... The food bank can be helpful but we have one that gives out old yucky food... Well, they wouldn't be digging in trash cans; maybe they can have a shower, a warm bed, also find a job and get on their feet.

Coming from someone who has had to use our food banks, the food quality is awful. A lot of the food is either almost expired, if not already. Also, to add fuel to the fire, a lot of the food that people take gets thrown away because they don't have a proper way to store it... We should ask about their situation, what they need, what would help them, not just get a name for the system and send them on their merry way. It goes so much deeper. Why do you think that person is homeless or hungry to begin with? They need help.

Many of the current food banks are handing out EXPIRED food. You shouldn't be forced to eat expired food just because you're income restricted... I know that picking through expired food makes me feel less respected as a member of the community.

A lot of senior citizens have to choose between eating and buying life saving drugs, this should not even be an issue in this country... I was without insurance when I had a stroke... I'm still getting bills in the thousands... The medication that I needed had to be purchased by someone else because I couldn't afford it.

It's hard worrying about how I'm gonna feed all these people with only one pound of meat that costs me 7 dollars... We need more canned food days and frozen meats... at the pantry. You can only go once a month for the essentials. Then they have... sweets every week. Not healthy.

We have several health issues that hopefully will work themselves out.... Having some changes made to our local schools and daycare centers have been a huge help. There is support for school needs and lunch programs.

One thing that would make a real difference... is being in a position where... my health is strong enough that I can actually enjoy that stability... Personal experiences with income dips... have shown how financial stress directly saps energy for health routines like consistent meals or walks... without reliable pay, even small health habits (like buying better food or gym time) get sidelined by survival mode.

I always provided health insurance for my family and I was able to keep them on my plan longer with the ACA and would think most parents find this a comfort... I would like to see healthcare, housing and food security for families with children... When people have food and housing they feel less desperate and safer which leads to less depression, anxiety and anger.

I would like to see more food pantries... One day my son... came to me and said, 'Mom, I just saw a family with a toddler looking through trash trying to find something to eat.' That hurts; nobody should go hungry, especially a child.

A lot of us senior citizens have to make choices between actually buying groceries or do without because of the high cost of medical help or medications... It would be a godsend. The seniors could breathe a little better and improve their daily life. Being able to buy the little extras like toilet paper or a garbage can is very satisfying

Inflation is seriously hurting the poor. We can't afford to survive and are going hungry... My community needs more funding to help with food banks. All the food they give is mostly garbage and you can't survive off of it.

I want state representatives to provide more options for those in need... so I'm not seeing people in my community struggle and have to decide between feeding their family or paying rent... I see a lot of ones in my area struggling to do all of it.

Eating healthy is hard when bad food costs less... We make more on one income to qualify for help, but we struggle. Changing the income thresholds for help... could really impact my family... It would mean less stress on the adults and would allow us to be more present with kids.

Increase support structures for families and children, like Soonercare, SNAP, TANF and childcare subsidies. ... I am seeing people having to stay on hold for hours and hours over multiple days just to renew their childcare assistence- often putting their jobs in jeopardy.

I can't work due to lack of an ID and my mother... currently isn't working due to having a broken arm... I just want easier access to food and groceries... If there was some way to pick out groceries from local food banks and have them delivered, for free, to our home.

Eat better, get in better shape... Prices are so d--- high. It affects what I buy. Healthier, organic is way more expensive... I have acreage I don't even use, that could possibly be turned into a community garden...

We need promotion and assistance with healthier food and to get rid of this junk that is poisoning us... When I started learning from a psychologist friend of mine about how much plastic is in our brains, it started to make me start thinking and researching, and it is very disturbing.

I hope that healthcare is more affordable and is able to cover more important issues. ... I hope that food will also become more affordable to better the health of myself and others. ... My neighbors have already started their own small food bank without help from major companies... I think us as a small town could do more things like that instead of constantly relying on those who cannot help us.

When one would rather have fresh fruit and fresh vegetables but can't afford to purchase them and haves to buy canned vegetables and canned fruit instead, I don't think that's fair. It certainly isn't as healthy for people. Better eating prevents future health issues.

I would love to see... help with basic needs such as food, as many people in my community have started to not have enough money to support their own families... This would mean less stress and even just being able to be healthier as a whole. Not having to be as worried about if they can afford food and bills for the month.
I would like to see no tax on fresh produce as I think this would help our state to have more affordable food options. ... Healthy food and focus on exercise would improve class performance.

I wish... people don't have to struggle to make a living... Help them out with money if you can, help him out with food, shelter... I know it's hard in this world to make it but if we all lean together we can make it.
I would like to see my community with less homelessness and poverty... I would reach out to help locate... resources... for residents for food, love and being there.

My current struggle is food. I’m a single mom working full time but I can’t qualify for food stamps... I make $15/hour and have 2 teenagers. We can’t afford food... the food stamps thing, it just needs to be looked into, penalize the liars getting a large amount and abusing the system and let the honest people be able to benefit and get help... It costs less to eat fast food than to buy groceries for one meal for my family.

They are stripping our benefits right and left. Our mental health care, our... medical benefits, food, our housing…where has it all gone? ... We can’t afford food at my house most weeks. We have beans and noodles to get by. ... I want to see this state have... better health care, especially mental health services. FUND that! People who are retired and over the age of 64 should not have to beg pain doctors for a minutia of the medication they need

We need better systems put in place to over come regional poverty... I would definitely say things such as EBT, WIC and Section 8. All things that help people that are in poverty. I believe this is where our tax dollars should be going.
Lowering prices would help the people of Oklahoma to end hunger... I've also had times where I didn't know when/if I was going to eat certain days.

There needs to be more of an emphasis on feeding people who can't feed themselves... I'm a real estate developer... and have somebody... working for me and they talk about... how they'll walk 2 miles to the store just so they can... get some food or medicine for the family because they don't have money for it.

I love to see people not have to worry about their next meal or commit a crime to provide a meal to take care of their family. ... We could start by growing our own fruits and vegetables... throughout the whole community... to bring us together as a unit.

Everyone should just come together on what to help others... Like when the food stamps was an issue... the community came together and provided food boxes and the school provided food bags for families to take home.

My health is not so good, so I would like to get that straightened out... I would like to see better health options and maybe look into repairing houses for homeless people instead of tearing them down, and lowering grocery prices.

I would like to see leaders actually hearing the people out and giving back into the community, such as food drives and even shelter for those in need... because people have no empathy anymore... if they put themselves in their shoes, they would get it and understand that some people just need some help.

I’m so tired of struggling to feed my kids because they have dealt with food insecurity their whole lives. Just like me. ... I know the food noise is always on and my kids are always hungry. There really isn't anything around here but fast food which isn't really healthy for you... If that changed, it would cut the rates down, obesity and things like that... and there wouldn't be so much poverty and struggle in the community.

A leader could show they care with activities that help your mental health state, also grocery store rides for those in need, that don't have cars or reliable resources... With that support, it will be easier to breathe and live... you can afford the necessities and the wants like celebrating kids birthdays or holiday gifts.

The one thing that I hope will be true in five to ten years... would be affordable healthy food options in stores... Achieving a better tomorrow would be easier if there were more programs for people who are financially struggling to pay their bills every month.
We want to have good and fresh food... the grocery store food looks old always... I would like to see... us get... clean food because what the heck and help the homeless more.

We need prescription drug coverage that doesn’t cost more than the drugs... I want to take care of myself. I do not want the government to take care of me. Some kind of middle ground would be helpful. I do pick up food from the monthly food bank.

I get $22 in food stamps a month. Just not enough to eat healthy... I eat government meals because I can’t afford healthy goods... The government meals that I have to eat are high in sodium which is not good.
I’ve known elderly who only get $24 in food stamps and no family to help them. That’s not enough to live off of.

We need better healthcare and infrastructure like food stamps and health insurance... to take care of the children we have. ... A lot of us are either at or below the poverty line. There needs to be help for those of us at risk for losing our homes, and who lack... food support. $6 a day is much too little.

I would like for them to make it so that we would not pay taxes on food purchases. That way people have access to a better quality of healthy types of foods... It seems like the food people can afford now needs to be replaced or added to healthier options.
It is difficult to keep up with raising prices... People will be able to buy their kids food instead of depend on the schools to provide food for their children through programs.

Make our food banks have better choices instead of freezer burnt meat... and take old buildings to house the homeless that can take thier pets... because some have no family except thier pets. So...they need to be together.
My man needs a job... We'd be more financially stable... I think all school lunches should be free.
We need more libraries and gyms, less fast food... Gyms help more with self improvement and confidence... We also need housing support and employment support for sure.
I hope medical insurance will be more accessible... You need toilet paper, food, water, soap; the basic needs are the highest priced things you buy.

I wish I didn’t have a penis, I wish I was a girl, but hey, can't do nothing about it now can I? ... This is because of gender dysphoria, depression and being broke... I would like to see anti trans laws get abolished, and to bring back food stamps... because hatefulness is wrong.

It would help with the government's make America healthy again agenda since steak, cheese and... whole milk is at the top of the government's new food pyramid... the top three things that are expensive in groceries.

We need to explain the importance of fresh local nutrition that will enhance the performance of our students as well as our residents... This would lead to healthier lifestyles and less medical attention. This would save our state government many dollars.

I just wish people that were having a hard time getting a job that are truly looking would be able to have some kind of resources to get some help... For example, help with bills or groceries or anything like that.
Being approved for foodstamps would help but unfortunately I don’t qualify. I’m not married and don’t have a job because I can’t afford daycare, so I have to stay home with my kids.

To achieve a better tomorrow, I would increase accessibility to individuals across the state who may not currently have access to things like healthcare, insurance, or are living in food insecure places... Not everyone is as fortunate as the next and I think it’s important to help those who may not have as much fortune because it will ultimately help our societies function.

Make sure laws are in place to help seniors and others as they age so they will not have to struggle and worry about the future... Not having good health care and not being able to afford food and shelter are struggles for lots of people.
More opportunities for citizens to receive the financial support we need and deserve... and funding for lower income families and food banks for citizens to receive for their families.

Stop spreading raw unfiltered humanure on the cattle pastures and open fields in rural Oklahoma... Poisoning the ground where the food is grown is not smart... Ground that has been contaminated with human waste cannot be grazed or plowed for decades. That is a long term contamination that costs the farmer every year that land is unusable.

More help for the homeless and hungry people of this community... As a country, we send more aid to countries that need medical, housing and food... and neglect our own... If you're gonna begin helping the needy, the homeless and the elderly, you have to start somewhere and why not close to where you live?
We need higher wages to help with the homeless and bringing the cost of goods down to where the lower class can afford groceries and the essentials ... If that happened, we would be eating better.
Families may not be able to get much food with plenty of nutritional value... With gas prices so high, that takes away from the budget to get food and to travel to the stores to get said food.
I want to see our local and state leaders... to feed the poor and protect the weak. ... No underage sex changes. Taking care of our seniors and our veterans.
I hope that food stamps and assistant programs like that would be easier to access... and that there would be more food support.
We need better resources for housing and food. They should focus on what is best for Oklahoma and not trying to impress our president.

Better healthcare coverage and support for lower income families. ... Then families would be able to afford health care that they currently canâ€™t and would not be in poverty. Children wouldnâ€™t be starving...

In all honesty, I should be on disability working part time and putting my health first...but I can't or we'd drown. I don't have the privilege of filling out the paperwork and waiting 2+ years to get approved... It feels the government wants to keep you trapped in this cycle. For example: I work X amount of overtime or get a $1.00 raise then the little assistance I do get, gets lowered or cut off... Change the requirements in TANF and SNAP benefits, so that getting raises and overtime will not affect your assistance so drastically and you can actually climb out of the hole and... break the cycle and succeed. 90% of the people on assistance, do NOT want to be on assistance. We just want to take care of our families and live...not survive, LIVE.

I want them to know that even though we don't fall under current income guidelines for assistance, that it doesn't mean we don't need it. We are severely struggling... It's hard to push through every day because every day is a question on how are we going to eat tonight... If more opportunities were available to past offenders... there would be less hunger and less poverty... It helps cut back on... state and government assistance needed. Fewer people needing food stamps, less people on TANF. Being able to afford healthcare.

The benefits extended to Oklahomans living in poverty are being revoked. The qualifications are becoming too stringent. As a 57-year-old, full-time student, I no longer even qualify for basic necessities like SNAP... At my age, I should be looking forward to retirement... Instead, I am embarking on a whole new career... I hope to make systematic changes in human services.

There is assistance, but we're past the threshold. Even though we're past it, it's still hard living in a 5 person family. What we earn just isn't enough even though we're "stable" financially... We may make more than the threshold, but since we're larger, it means we're equivalent to a family who qualifies for assistance.

There needs to be better mental health awareness, services, and access... and assistance for families or single mothers who are trying to achieve independence, especially when displaying the desire to not be on assistance. I have a niece who is a single mother of three... she is trying to go back to school in order to provide for her children... Her daycare is ridiculous and the co-pays expected lead to choosing to pay for daycare or pay for food. It’s a continual catch 22.

I would love to see some revisions done to the government benefits system. I believe if the state employed a ton of counselors who would come alongside those receiving benefits, help them budget, give them guidance and encouragement... those people in the system would have enough support to dig their way out of the trenches... When you have no vision or drive or hope for what your life could look like, it's hard to stay motivated to keep making good decisions. People... who feel like they're being kicked when they're already down don't have the strength to even begin looking for those resources to be able to approach someone and ask for help.

We need more resources for people like me and my family because they say there are programs, but when you need them they're not there at all. ... My house was burned to the ground and I was denied help because it wasn't a natural disaster.

Spending what money I could save on medication is the reason it is hard to save... I would like to see lower health insurance cost for medications and a higher stricter requirement for EBT and government funding.

The government takes and takes from the workers that keep this country going and hands it off to others that... use welfare as a lifestyle. My own personal experience is what is shaping my opinion. I work 72 hours a week... while raising two boys and trying to keep a roof over their heads we simply cannot afford it. This is wrong; all we do is work til we die and have very little to show for it.
More help for people who need it like me.... I've asked for assistance for years and kept being told I made $20 too much. I understand limitations but it really is hard.

I pay $400 a month for my insurances as I don’t qualify any longer for SoonerCare because of my income... They don’t incorporate expenses when asking for income so people like me can’t buy much groceries... I would like to see more middle class workers receive help.

I started working at 14 and worked most of my life and raised 4 children by myself. When I had to retire due to cancer, I had to move in with one of my children because I lost my house and don’t make enough to live alone... My child barely makes it and because I live with him there is help I don’t qualify for. So there is always living from check to check and no one gets ahead.

When I was a young adult, they changed AFDC to TANF and made some changes that were supposed to save money... and benefit the state. 30 years later, I’ve yet to see any changes or benefits and would like to know where all the money have went.
I wish we could get a government subsidy to install a safe room... I'd like to see the state reduce entitlement programs but also include a program to provide affordable storm shelters.

I believe there are enough homeless shelters to provide for the homeless but not enough funding. There’s programs for families, but sometimes those families don’t need things from Food Angels and there’s no checks to verify if they do or not.

Living on a set income and being disabled is not fun. ... When I ask for help, they say, 'You make to much money and we can’t help you'. I've had that happen a lot. I'm unable to work because of the pain I’m in all the time.

It is not easy getting help from the state or from your tribes. If one person works and the other... can't find work, that one person makes too much by like 40 dollars to get help. So they do bad things to get what the family needs cause it's a cycle, and with out better help it won't ever change.
We need more opportunities for those of us that barely make enough to eat but too much for any assistance. We are not asking for handouts, just a way to make it in the world.

I would like to see the state stop being so strict when it comes to a family having to get on welfare... People might start looking out for one another in their communities if they weren’t ridiculed because they have to be on welfare or disability.

I would like to see fewer welfare programs and make people more accountable for their own lives... Take responsibility for your self and support your self... so we the tax payers do not have to support you.

I think we need more proactive programs to get people off social services and into the workforce... Social services should require a certain amount of effort to become job eligible in order to access the services... with a few exceptions for people who are truly physically or mentally unable to hold any type of job.
I would like to see a crack down on the ones on welfare to have to take drug tests... A welfare drug test would clear that up and make them get a job... You fail a test, you're off of welfare.
Offering free benefits without proper checks and balances only hinders our spend and focus... We have people who mislead or fraudulently abuse the system and use those resources as well.
We need tougher punishments and more rehabilitation for criminals... teach them about God. Make them work and quit giving so much free money to deadbeats.

There is a lot of violence here that I didn't see when I was growing up... I'd like to see an increase in police presence in bad areas to clean up neighborhoods and keep gangs locked up... and let people open carry and not be charged for defending themselves.

The... highway human trafficking is a really big issue... we rank high in highway trafficking... We need more awareness on cold cases and crime statewide. I've noticed throughout the Oklahoma cold cases database that county wide we are missing a lot of information but beyond that, no one even knows the names of those who are missing... unless it reaches the Oklahoma news... it seemingly never gets the attention that it would need to potentially be solved.
