I want my platform to be used as a voice of advocacy for abuse survivors and a call for judicial reform... I want to see uniform sentencing for those who prey on children and teens. The Morris and Butler cases left survivors with no sense of justice... Complaints from people in my community give me the impression our voices aren't being heard... We want to be able to live freely and not worry about other ideologies creeping in to make life more miserable.

I would like to see harsher prison time sentences for those who abuse animals... and a background check before owning a pet of any kind... There was a male that didn't like cats in my area; he poisoned them... I found one he... killed. It was a stray cat that I befriended... He never was caught.

Putting a real effort into cleaning up neighborhoods. North Tulsa is absolutely frightening to drive through... We really need to crack down on drug sales... pills, fentanyl, and meth... we have quite the homeless problem here in Tulsa... A lot of them need mental help... and some of the homeless ones can be quite dangerous... We ignore what we don't want to look at and that is the mentally ill homeless people... if there was something I could do like volunteer work or help in some way, I would. I just don't have any answers.

I'd like to see it not be illegal to be "homeless." ... These new statutes making the unhoused citizens either chose jail or rehab borders on some form of government that is not democracy. I have struggled to keep a roof my entire life and lived without one many times. It's difficult enough to survive, without laws like that.

I already volunteer at a 7 month addiction recovery program... and we make a huge difference. I would like you to fund Christ-based long term (7-12 months) addiction recovery centers... This will strengthen and bring back many families and give children back parents who are able to raise them properly.

Everyone would be more involved and vigilant when it comes to crime and safety in their areas... I've seen first hand on a daily basis the effect of improper care for mentally ill individuals. They are admitted into a care facility, treated for a short time then released out into the streets to fend for themselves.

I would like to see the homeless people not be treated like criminals. I myself have been homeless for a few years off and on and I was always looked down on and treated like I was some criminal... Instead of telling them to leave places they are resting at, give them the option to go to a place that should be made for this situation.

There should be less criminal justice action against the homeless people. They really aren't doing anything and they really aren't hurting a whole lot of people... I've seen the way that they're clearing out the camps on the side of the highway and putting up the structures that are uncomfortable for them to sit on or sleep on.

I hope that... people open their eyes and realize what's going on around here. People need to start caring and helping instead of killing and putting each other down... but we can't change everyone... Some just like to do harm and evil, and those people, I think, should be murdered in my opinion. They would not ever see the light of day... But for others, just cuz they look dirty, stink, maybe do drugs, some want to be fixed. We can fix that by giving them counseling, giving them therapy, someone just giving their time to be there for them and talk to them and giving them courage and inspiration that they can do it.

Sending individuals struggling with drug addiction to jail or prison resembles enrolling them in a criminal academy... I recall the time I spent in prison. Instead of finding rehabilitation and therapeutic support, I ended up surrounded by individuals entrenched in illegal activities... What I truly craved was a compassionate space where I could receive the help that would enable me to heal and rebuild my life.

I would like to see the state stop taking people's kids and placing them with strangers... offer more substance abuse treatment facilities before taking a parent's rights... to let them know that they have family who cares and deeply loves them instead of thinking all through life that nobody loved nor cared for them.

We have to stop the fentanyl scourge at the source... But you can’t cure addiction in 28 days in a clinic... We need to investigate the much lower recidivism rate of long-term Christ-based addiction recovery... I have seen men who were homeless for years accept Christ... get their families back and become real fathers to their children and real husbands to their wives.

We need less money spent on crimes that are non-violent. Too many misdemeanor crimes lead to incarceration... I know so many people locked up for years over drug-related crimes. First we have to pay to take care of each person locked up; then we have to finance the courts.

My community is going through a tough time right now. The judiciary system is failing us.... We are having problems with judges letting guilty criminals off with probation or a slap on the wrist. The community doesn’t understand how this is happening over and over with the same judge.

I’m a felon and it’s hard to get a steady job... I would like the justice system to be audited and investigated... so it can actually make progress instead of throwing people in prison and making money off the incarcerated people like they were cattle... Drug laws... need to be decriminalized... so they could actually receive help rather than being labeled a criminal which will keep them a criminal usually for the rest of their lives.
I started a nonprofit to help with community safety... We need compassion for the forgotten and overlooked. Don’t criminalize homelessness.

The overall homelessness presence can shift the feeling of feeling safe in my community because some have mental illnesses and or are on drugs. … Maybe incarcerating them or, even better, finding a permanent place to reside them until they get a job.

A large part of our population here has an extensive criminal history... Better enforcement of laws and better programs for others struggling with abuse or addictions would help a lot of people get back on their feet.

I hope that Oklahoma was the most popular state for rehabilitation of addicts instead of the most popular for woman incarcerations... I am currently in this situation and before this I would have never thought this could be me. Then it’s like, okay, I’m homeless now; how do I get off drugs if I’m homeless?

I would like us to have a new district attorney here in Ardmore, Oklahoma, Carter County because... the current one lets child predators... walk... If they actually lock up the monsters... they need to do more to make families feel safe, like do their actual job.

I would like to see more enforcement and a strict stance on illegal drugs... to provide an atmosphere free of fear from crime that accompanies illegal drug use. Offer remedies such as a treatment plan for drug offenders and truth in sentencing when it comes to violent crimes.

We have to do something about the homeless instead of just moving them around or putting them in jail... They made it a crime to be homeless... We need to train our police force on how to deal with mental health.
I see homeless people literally crawling the streets... people having outbursts in the streets and just being arrested and not helped.

I would like to see marijuana recreationally legalized... People would notice a decrease in crime and... a general feel of happiness and well-being... because if everybody smokes a joint then nobody is fighting over dumb s---.

We need stronger enforcement of crimes. We should not let individuals get away with crime because of their family ties. Jessie Butler should never have been treated as a child and released while victims have lifelong injuries.
Stop taking people to jail for petty drug crimes.... It would better the whole community if the drug addicts got help instead of being sent to prison where there's only more drugs.

There are bad people like drug users, thieves ... that go to jail then get out to hurt someone else ... over and over. They need to stay in jail and other people who don’t need to be in jail should get out and get help that they need. ... The ones who need help ... and genuinely are trying should not be thrown in with the bad dangerous people.
We need to stop overpacking prisons. Mental health is needed. If the person isn't violent, why cage them like an animal?

People out here need... jobs that understand people make mistakes. It's how you come back from those mistakes that shows the person you really are. People feel they have no other choice... so they do some bad things to make the money to feed the family's... it's cycles and with out better help it won't ever change... I know for a fact background check companies do not have the right and true information... I know my back ground check has things on it that ain't even right... and it hurts people... some make mistakes at 18 and it's still there in their mid 20s and they stayed out of trouble.

It's harder for me because of my past... We need to help for more people who has no one to help... a way to make money without an I.D. because they lose them a lot. It's due to the thefts of food and toiletries and merchandise.

You get punished for life for one mistake... I'm a felon and it's hard to find a job. You are punished forever... it was 29 years ago and I still am being punished and still can't get a good job... I want a pardon from the governor... a good job, a chance at a normal life, help to get back on my feet... Records should be dropped when your depbt to society is paid.

I want to see more second chance opportunities for our past criminals... My husband served his time behind bars, yet he still serves time while not behind them by suffering from not getting opportunities... Many of them took classes while behind bars and are now trained in many fields... and good talent is just being washed away because they are not given chances like everyone else.

I hope my background will be expunged like it's supposed to be... I literally fell in the crack. Second chance companies told me they didn't have to hire me cuz I didn't have a felony. Regular companies turned me away cuz I couldn't pass a background check. For seven plus years I've struggled trying to find employment.

I feel like if our felonies are 10+ years old and we haven’t gotten another felony, it should not stop us from getting a job that we would like to get. If we had more job opportunities, we would get less people on welfare and food stamps… I feel like a person like me is very consistent. At my last job I was there 10 years. My last landlord I have was for 10 years… if people look at my past as far as my work history and my rental history and not my background, then that should matter, not the law that I broke when I was young and dumb.

My husband went to prison on drug charges so now he’s a felon. He can’t get a good job even with his master's degree. ... He shouldn’t be looked at as a felon the rest of his life because of an addiction he had never gotten treatment for. ... We should make it to where Oklahoma is known as a second chance state.

My son refuses to come home... because we are too poor to pay for counsel for his warrant that involves marijuana from 12 plus years ago... A BETTER TOMORROW WOULD BE FOR MY SON AND FAMILY TO COME HOME AND NOT FEAR PRISON AND LIVE FREELY... Since Oklahoma has become a legal marijuana state, wipe all records prior to the year it became legal... IT'S TRAGIC TO HAVE A HISTORY WHEN IT'S LEGAL NOW.

I support the state and the sealing of criminal records and offer more first time buisness loans to felons. We want to have business because we are taught in prison to be work oriented. I want to continue that skill... I would love to be a member of my community.

I would like to see... additional programs to aid in traffic tickets and or warrants from those tickets so it doesn’t end up affecting the individual negatively in the long run... I currently have an excessive amount of traffic warrants making it difficult to secure employment and be able to have feasible transportation to and from said places of employment.

I feel that our criminal justice system is broken and it is a revolving door that traps people... I have personally been caught in that revolving door myself. Ever since I was 18... I have been under their control... and constantly in debt to them.

I would hope... that the mistakes I made in my past are not still making it impossible to support myself... Maybe the state can come up with a program that will help me with my Texas felony... so mistakes I made 5 years ago are not still causing me missed job opportunities. I am willing to admit I was wrong and have kept my nose clean for 5 years; hopefully there’s a light at the end of the tunnel.

There’s people out who try their d--- best to be the best they can, aka like me, and yet have to fight through back and beyond just to be able to see their kids... Give them chances to prove themselves... Don’t keep making them do the most. It’s wrong.

I’m a felon and it’s hard to get a steady job... I would like the justice system to be audited and investigated... so it can actually make progress instead of throwing people in prison and making money off the incarcerated people like they were cattle... Drug laws... need to be decriminalized... so they could actually receive help rather than being labeled a criminal which will keep them a criminal usually for the rest of their lives.

Right now these laws are robbing people of their chance of the American dream. ...when you're regulated to where you can live, it creates problems with being closer to your support group like your family. ...if you can't go to the park with your family, you really can't bond with your family at the park. ...treating people like monsters can actually make them act like monsters as opposed to what you really want, which is to make a safe environment for everyone.
I plan to have a restaurant running to... give job opportunities to felons. I believe it best benefits the community.
It would help if jobs did better with people's criminal history because that feels like a barrier. And without that obstacle, I feel like more jobs would be available.
I hope I have enough money to make my kids and grandkids never have to stress paying for things... It would also be easier if felons could get decent jobs.

The future would seem more bright if possible employers were only able to pull up felonies on a background check... I believe it would open up more opportunities with higher paying jobs which would help the economy. There would be less need for government assistance and hopefully decrease the amount of homeless people in the US.

I would like to see the blanket strict sex offender laws to change because the law as it is makes it almost nearly impossible for them to be able to find housing or employment... It would be the... deserved second chance that they needed to be able to become successful members of society once again.

We need jobs for people that have been in trouble. Some people do have a past but have straightened up; they just need a chance... If they had that, more people would feel good about their self. They would... feel like more people would believe in them and not against them.
It doesn't matter how hard you work; if you've even been in trouble or have a record, they wonâ€™t give you the time of day. People change.

I would like for the laws on violent offenders (not sex related) who haven’t offended again to be reworked so that they can get their licenses back... Without the “violent offenders” label, more people would work. There would be less stress on welfare systems.

I would like our state to start giving felons the option to get their gun rights back... I just feel like it’s everyone’s right to have a firearm, so why being a felon would have anything to do to impede that right is beyond me.
Minimum wage is entirely too low. No one can raise a family on minimum wage, especially if they’re a felon and can’t get any other job. Please fix it.
We need more higher paying jobs for felonies, where we can get ahead.
